1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: ALIF LAM RA. These are the verses of the Book and the perspicuous oration.

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: Alif-Lam-Ra; these are verses of the Book and the bright Qur’an.

1.author: Arberry
1.text: Alif Lam Ra. Those are the signs of the Book and of a manifest Koran.

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: Alif. Lam. Ra. These are the verses of the Book, and a Qur'an luminous.

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Alif-Lam-Ra. [These letters are one of the miracles of the Quran, and none but Allah (Alone) knows their meanings]. These are the Verses of the Book, and a plain Quran.

1.author: Itani
1.text: Alif, Lam, Ra. These are the Verses of the Book; a Quran that makes things clear.

1.author: Maududi
1.text: Alif. Lam. Ra'. These are the verses of the Book, and a Clear Qur'an.

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: Alif-Lam-Ra. These are Ayat of the Book and a plain Qur'an.

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: Alif. Lam. Ra. These are verses of the Scripture and a plain Reading.

1.author: Qarai
1.text: Alif, Lam, Ra. These are the signs of the Book and a manifest Quran.

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: AlifLaamRa. Those are the verses of the Book, the Clear Koran.

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: Alif, Lam, Ra. These are the verses of the Book and a clear Qur'an.

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: Alif. Lam. Ra. These are the verses of the Book and the glorious Quran.

1.author: Shakir
1.text: Alif Lam Ra. These are the verses of the Book and (of) a Quran that makes (things) clear.

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: Alif Lam Ra. These are the verses of a clear Book, the Quran.

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: A. L. R. These are the Ayats of Revelation,- of a Qur'an that makes things clear.

1.author: Transliteration
1.text: Aliflamra tilka ayatu alkitabi waquranin mubeenin

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: The unbelievers would haply like to wish that they had submitted (and become Muslim).

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: The disbelievers will ardently wish that if only they had been Muslims! (At the time of death / in their graves / on the Day of Resurrection.)

2.author: Arberry
2.text: Perchance the unbelievers will wish that they had surrendered:

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: Oft times would those who disbelieve fain that they had been Muslims.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: Perhaps (often) will those who disbelieve wish that they were Muslims [those who have submitted themselves to Allah's Will in Islam Islamic Monotheism, this will be on the Day of Resurrection when they will see the disbelievers going to Hell and the Muslims going to Paradise].

2.author: Itani
2.text: Perhaps those who disbelieve would like to become Muslims.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: Soon will the time come when the unbelievers will wish they were Muslims.

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: How much would those who disbelieved wish that they had been Muslims.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: It may be that those who disbelieve wish ardently that they were Muslims.

2.author: Qarai
2.text: Much will the faithless wish that they had been Muslims.

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: It could be that those who disbelieve will wish that they were Muslims.

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: Perhaps those who disbelieve will wish that they had been Muslims.

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: How strongly the unbelievers will wish that they had been Muslims.

2.author: Shakir
2.text: Often will those who disbelieve wish that they had been Muslims.

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: A time will surely come when those who are bent on denying the truth will wish that they had surrendered themselves to God,

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: Again and again will those who disbelieve, wish that they had bowed (to Allah's will) in Islam.

2.author: Transliteration
2.text: Rubama yawaddu allatheena kafaroo law kanoo muslimeena

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: Leave them to feast and revel, beguiled by hope; they will come to know soon.

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: Leave them to eat and enjoy, and for aspiration to involve them in play – so they will shortly come to know.

3.author: Arberry
3.text: leave them to eat, and to take their joy, and to be bemused by hope; certainly they will soon know!

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: Leave them thou to eat and to enjoy, and let vain hope divert them; presently they will know.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: Leave them to eat and enjoy, and let them be preoccupied with (false) hope. They will come to know!

3.author: Itani
3.text: Leave them to eat, and enjoy, and be lulled by hope. They will find out.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: Leave them to eat and enjoy life and let false hopes amuse them. They will soon come to know.

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: Leave them to eat and enjoy, and let them be preoccupied with (false) hope. They will come to know!

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: Let them eat and enjoy life, and let (false) hope beguile them. They will come to know!

3.author: Qarai
3.text: Leave them to eat and enjoy and to be diverted by longings. Soon they will know.

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: Let them eat and enjoy; and let their hopes deceive them, soon they shall know.

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: Let them eat and enjoy themselves and be diverted by [false] hope, for they are going to know.

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: (Muhammad), leave them alone to eat and enjoy themselves and let their desires deceive them; they will soon know (the Truth).

3.author: Shakir
3.text: Leave them that they may eat and enjoy themselves and (that) hope may beguile them, for they will soon know.

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: so leave them to eat and enjoy themselves and let them be beguiled by vain hopes; for soon they will realise [the truth].

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: Leave them alone, to enjoy (the good things of this life) and to please themselves: let (false) hope amuse them: soon will knowledge (undeceive them).

3.author: Transliteration
3.text: Tharhum yakuloo wayatamattaAAoo wayulhihimu alamalu fasawfa yaAAlamoona

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: Not one habitation have We destroyed but at the time determined for it.

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: And every dwelling that We destroyed, had a known decree for it.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: Never a city have We destroyed, but it had a known decree,

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: And We have not destroyed a town but there was therefor a decree known.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: And never did We destroy a township but there was a known decree for it.

4.author: Itani
4.text: We have never destroyed a town unless it had a set time.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: Whenever We destroyed a town, a definite term had previously been decreed for it.

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: And never did We destroy a township but there was a known decree for it.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: And We destroyed no township but there was a known decree for it.

4.author: Qarai
4.text: We did not destroy any town but that it had a known term.

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: We never destroyed a village whose term had not already been decreed.

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: And We did not destroy any city but that for it was a known decree.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: We never destroyed any town without pre-ordaining the fate of its people.

4.author: Shakir
4.text: And never did We destroy a town but it had a term made known.

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: We have never destroyed a township without a definite decree having been issued;

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: Never did We destroy a population that had not a term decreed and assigned beforehand.

4.author: Transliteration
4.text: Wama ahlakna min qaryatin illa walaha kitabun maAAloomun

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: No people can hasten or delay the term already fixed for them.

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: No group may advance its appointed promise nor postpone it.

5.author: Arberry
5.text: and no nation outstrips its term, nor do they put it back.

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: No community precedeth the term thereof nor doth it fall behind.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: No nation can anticipate its term, nor delay it.

5.author: Itani
5.text: No nation can bring its time forward, nor can they delay it.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: No people can outstrip the term for its destruction nor can it delay it.

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: No nation can advance its term, nor delay it.

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: No nation can outstrip its term nor can they lag behind.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: No nation can advance its time nor can it defer it.

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: No nation can outstrip its term, nor do they put it back.

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: No nation will precede its term, nor will they remain thereafter.

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: Every nation can only live for the time appointed for it.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: No people can hasten on their doom nor can they postpone (it).

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: no people can forestall their doom, nor can they delay it.

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: Neither can a people anticipate its term, nor delay it.

5.author: Transliteration
5.text: Ma tasbiqu min ommatin ajalaha wama yastakhiroona

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: And yet they say: "You, to whom this Exposition has been sent are surely possessed of the Devil.

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: And they said, “O you upon whom the Qur’an has been sent, you are indeed insane.”

6.author: Arberry
6.text: They say: 'Thou, upon whom the Remembrance is sent down, thou art assuredly possessed!

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: And they say: O thou unto whom the Admonition hath been sent down! verily thou art possessed.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: And they say: "O you (Muhammad SAW) to whom the Dhikr (the Quran) has been sent down! Verily, you are a mad man.

6.author: Itani
6.text: And they said, “O you who received the message, you are insane.”

6.author: Maududi
6.text: They say: "O you to whom the Admonition has been revealed, you are surely crazed.

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: And they say: "O you (Muhammad) to whom the Dhikr (the Qur'an) has been revealed! Verily, you are a madman!

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: And they say: O thou unto whom the Reminder is revealed, lo! thou art indeed a madman!

6.author: Qarai
6.text: They said, ‘O you, to whom the Reminder has been sent down, you are indeed crazy.

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: They say: 'You to whom the Remembrance is sent down, you are indeed mad.

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: And they say, "O you upon whom the message has been sent down, indeed you are mad.

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: (The unbelievers have said), "You to whom the Quran has been revealed are insane.

6.author: Shakir
6.text: And they say: O you to whom the Reminder has been revealed! you are most surely insane:

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: They say, "You to whom the Reminder [the Quran] has been sent down, you are surely possessed.

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: They say: "O thou to whom the Message is being revealed! truly thou art mad (or possessed)!

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Waqaloo ya ayyuha allathee nuzzila AAalayhi alththikru innaka lamajnoonun

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: If you are a man of truth, why can't you bring the angels to us?"

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: “Why do you not bring angels to us, if you are truthful?”

7.author: Arberry
7.text: Why dost thou not bring the angels unto us, if thou speakest truly?'

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: Why bringest thou not angels unto us if thou art of the truthtellers!

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: "Why do you not bring angels to us if you are of the truthful ones?"

7.author: Itani
7.text: Why do you not bring us the angels, if you are truthful?”

7.author: Maududi
7.text: Why do you not bring down angels upon us if you are indeed truthful?"

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: "Why do you not bring angels to us if you are of the truthful"

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: Why bringest thou not angels unto us, if thou art of the truthful?

7.author: Qarai
7.text: Why do you not bring us the angels should you be truthful?!’

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: Why do you not bring down the angels, if what you say is true'

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: Why do you not bring us the angels, if you should be among the truthful?"

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: Why do you not bring down the angels if what you say is true".

7.author: Shakir
7.text: Why do you not bring to us the angels if you are of the truthful ones?

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: Why do you not bring down the angels upon us, if you are truthful?"

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: "Why bringest thou not angels to us if it be that thou hast the Truth?"

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: Law ma tateena bialmalaikati in kunta mina alssadiqeena

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: But then We never send the angels down save with the purpose (of enforcing their doom), after which they will not be given more respite.

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: We do not send down angels without reason, and if the angels descend they would not get any respite!

8.author: Arberry
8.text: We send not down the angels, save with truth;

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: We send not the angels down save with judgment, and then they would not be respited.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: We send not the angels down except with the truth (i.e. for torment, etc.), and in that case, they (the disbelievers) would have no respite!

8.author: Itani
8.text: We do not send the angels down except with reason, and they will not be held back.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: We do not send down the angels (in frivolity); and when We do send them down, We do so with Truth; then people are granted no respite.

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: We do not send the angels down except with the truth, and in that case, they (the disbelievers) would have no respite!

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: We send not down the angels save with the Fact, and in that case (the disbelievers) would not be tolerated.

8.author: Qarai
8.text: We do not send down the angels except with due reason, and then they will not be granted any respite.

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: We do not send angels except with the truth. Then they shall have no respite.

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: We do not send down the angels except with truth; and the disbelievers would not then be reprieved.

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: We do not send down angels except for a genuine purpose, at which time none will be given any further respite.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: We do not send the angels but with truth, and then they would not be respited.

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: But We send down the angels only to bring justice and then they will not be reprieved.

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: We send not the angels down except for just cause: if they came (to the ungodly), behold! no respite would they have!

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: Ma nunazzilu almalaikata illa bialhaqqi wama kanoo ithan munthareena

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: We have sent down this Exposition, and We will guard it.

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: Indeed We have sent down the Qur’an, and indeed We Ourselves surely are its Guardians. (This is one of the miracles of the Qur’an – no one has been able to change even one letter of its text, despite every effort. It has remained in its original form since the 6th Century (A. D) and will remain so forever. Other Holy Books such as the Torah and the Bible have lost their originality.)

9.author: Arberry
9.text: then they would not be respited. It is We who have sent down the Remembrance, and We watch over it.

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: Verily We! it is We who have revealed the Admonition, and verily We are the guardians thereof.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: Verily We: It is We Who have sent down the Dhikr (i.e. the Quran) and surely, We will guard it (from corruption).

9.author: Itani
9.text: Surely We revealed the Message, and We will surely preserve it.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: As for the Admonition, indeed it is We Who have revealed it and it is indeed We Who are its guardians.

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: Verily, We, it is We Who revealed the Dhikr (i.e. the Qur'an) and surely We will guard it (from corruption).

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: Lo! We, even We, reveal the Reminder, and lo! We verily are its Guardian.

9.author: Qarai
9.text: Indeed We have sent down the Reminder, and indeed We will preserve it.

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: It is We who sent down the Koran, and We watch over it.

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: Indeed, it is We who sent down the Qur'an and indeed, We will be its guardian.

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: We Ourselves have revealed the Quran and We are its Protectors.

9.author: Shakir
9.text: Surely We have revealed the Reminder and We will most surely be its guardian.

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: It is We who have sent down the Reminder and We will, most surely, safeguard it.

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: We have, without doubt, sent down the Message; and We will assuredly guard it (from corruption).

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Inna nahnu nazzalna alththikra wainna lahu lahafithoona

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: We had also sent apostles to people of earlier persuasions.

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: And indeed We sent Noble Messengers before you, to the former nations.

10.author: Arberry
10.text: Indeed, We sent Messengers before thee, among the factions of the ancients,

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: And assuredly We have sent apostles before thee among the sects of the ancients.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: Indeed, We sent Messengers before you (O Muhammad SAW) amongst the sects (communities) of old.

10.author: Itani
10.text: We sent others before you, to the former communities.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: (O Muhammad), certainly We did send Messengers before you among the nations which have gone by.

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: Indeed, We sent (Messengers) before you (O Muhammad) amongst the earlier communities.

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: We verily sent (messengers) before thee among the factions of the men of old.

10.author: Qarai
10.text: Certainly We sent [apostles] before you to former communities,

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: We have sent forth before you Messengers among the factions of the ancients.

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: And We had certainly sent [messengers] before you, [O Muhammad], among the sects of the former peoples.

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: We sent Messengers to the ancient people who lived before you.

10.author: Shakir
10.text: And certainly We sent (apostles) before you among the nations of yore.

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: We sent messengers before you to the previous peoples,

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: We did send messengers before thee amongst the religious sects of old:

10.author: Transliteration
10.text: Walaqad arsalna min qablika fee shiyaAAi alawwaleena

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: But never once an apostle came to them at whom they did not scoff.

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: And never does a Noble Messenger come to them, but they mock at him.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: and not a single Messenger came to them, but they mocked at him;

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: And not an apostle came unto them but at him they were wont to mock.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: And never came a Messenger to them but they did mock him.

11.author: Itani
11.text: But no messenger came to them, but they ridiculed him.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: And whenever a Messenger came to them, they never failed to mock him.

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: And there never came a Messenger to them but that they mocked him.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: And never came there unto them a messenger but they did mock him.

11.author: Qarai
11.text: and there did not come to them any apostle but that they used to deride him.

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: But no Messenger came to them except he was mocked.

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: And no messenger would come to them except that they ridiculed him.

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: No Messenger went to them whom they did not mock.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: And there never came an apostle to them but they mocked him.

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: but there was never a messenger who came to them but they mocked him:

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: But never came a messenger to them but they mocked him.

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: Wama yateehim min rasoolin illa kanoo bihi yastahzioona

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: We place in the hearts of sinners (disbelief).

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: This is how We make the mocking* enter the hearts of the guilty. (* The acts of disbelief.)

12.author: Arberry
12.text: even so We cause it to enter into the hearts of the sinners --

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: Even so we make a way for it in the hearts of the culprits.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: Thus do We let it (polytheism and disbelief) enter into the hearts of the Mujrimun [criminals, polytheists, pagans, etc. (because of their mockery at the Messengers)].

12.author: Itani
12.text: Thus We slip it into the hearts of the guilty.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: Even so We make a way for it (that is, the Admonition) in the hearts of the culprits (like a hot rod);

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: Thus We allow it to enter the hearts of the guilty.

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: Thus do We make it traverse the hearts of the guilty:

12.author: Qarai
12.text: That is how We let it pass through the hearts of the guilty:

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: So We make it enter the hearts of the sinners.

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: Thus do We insert denial into the hearts of the criminals.

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: This is how We cause the hearts of the guilty ones to behave.

12.author: Shakir
12.text: Thus do We make it to enter into the hearts of the guilty;

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: thus We cause this [habit of mocking] to enter into the hearts of the sinful.

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: Even so do we let it creep into the hearts of the sinners -

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: So, they will not believe in it: The example of former people is there.

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: They do not believe in him, and the tradition of earlier nations has passed.

13.author: Arberry
13.text: they believe not in it, though the wont of the ancients is already gone.

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: They believe not therein, and already the example of the ancients hath gone forth.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: They would not believe in it (the Quran), and already the example of (Allah's punishment of) the ancients (who disbelieved) has gone forth.

13.author: Itani
13.text: They do not believe in it, though the ways of the ancients have passed away.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: they do not believe in it. This has been the wont of people of this kind from ancient times.

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: They would not believe in it (the Qur'an); and already the example of the ancients has gone forth.

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: They believe not therein, though the example of the men of old hath gone before.

13.author: Qarai
13.text: they do not believe in it, and the precedent of the ancients has already passed.

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: They do not believe in it even though the practice of the ancients has already gone.

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: They will not believe in it, while there has already occurred the precedent of the former peoples.

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: They do not believe in the Truth and they exactly follow the tradition of the ancient (unbelievers)

13.author: Shakir
13.text: They do not believe in it, and indeed the example of the former people has already passed.

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: They will not believe in it, though they have before them the example of former peoples,

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: That they should not believe in the (Message); but the ways of the ancients have passed away.

13.author: Transliteration
13.text: La yuminoona bihi waqad khalat sunnatu alawwaleena

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: Even if We open a door in the heavens and they ascend through it in broad daylight,

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: And if We open for them a gate in the heavens, to ascend it through the day –

14.author: Arberry
14.text: Though We opened to them a gate in heaven, and still they mounted through it,

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: And if We opened upon them a door of the heaven, and they passed the day mounting thereto.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: And even if We opened to them a gate from the heaven and they were to continue ascending thereto,

14.author: Itani
14.text: Even if We opened for them a gateway into the sky, and they began to ascend through it.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: If We were even to open for them a way to the heavens, and they could continually climb up to it in broad daylight,

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: And even if We opened to them a gate to the heavens and they were to continue ascending through it (all day long).

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: And even if We opened unto them a gate of heaven and they kept mounting through it,

14.author: Qarai
14.text: Were We to open for them a gate of the sky, so that they could go on ascending through it,

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: If We opened a gate in the heaven and they kept ascending through it,

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: And [even] if We opened to them a gate from the heaven and they continued therein to ascend,

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: Had We opened a door for them in the sky through which they could easily pass,

14.author: Shakir
14.text: And even if We open to them a gateway of heaven, so that they ascend into it all the while,

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: and even if We opened to them a door from heaven, and they began ascending through it,

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: Even if We opened out to them a gate from heaven, and they were to continue (all day) ascending therein,

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: Walaw fatahna AAalayhim baban mina alssamai fathalloo feehi yaAArujoona

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: They will say all the same: "Our eyes were dazed (and clouded over). We were a people ensorcelled."

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: Even then they would say, “Our sights have been hypnotised – in fact, a magic spell has been cast upon us.”

15.author: Arberry
15.text: yet would they say, 'Our eyes have been dazzled; nay, we are a people bewitched!'

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: They would surely say: intoxicated have been our sights; aye! we are a people enchanted.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: They would surely say: "Our eyes have been (as if) dazzled. Nay, we are a people bewitched."

15.author: Itani
15.text: They would still say, “Our eyes are hallucinating; in fact, we are people bewitched.”

15.author: Maududi
15.text: they would still have said: "Surely our eyes have been dazzled; rather, we have been enchanted."

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: They would surely say (in the evening): "Our eyes have been (as if) dazzled (we have not seen any angel or heaven). Nay, we are a people bewitched."

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: They would say: Our sight is wrong - nay, but we are folk bewitched.

15.author: Qarai
15.text: they would surely say, ‘Indeed a spell has been cast on our eyes; indeed, we are a bewitched lot.’

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: still they would say: 'Our eyes were dazzled; truly, we must have been a bewitched people'

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: They would say, "Our eyes have only been dazzled. Rather, we are a people affected by magic."

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: they would have said, "Our eyes are bewildered and we have been affected by magic."

15.author: Shakir
15.text: They would certainly say: Only our eyes have been covered over, rather we are an enchanted people.

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: they would still say, "Our eyes have been dazzled. We are bewitched."

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: They would only say: "Our eyes have been intoxicated: Nay, we have been bewitched by sorcery."

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: Laqaloo innama sukkirat absaruna bal nahnu qawmun mashooroona

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: We have placed the signs of the Zodiac in the sky, and decked it out for those who can see;

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: And indeed We created towers in the skies, and beautified it for the beholders.

16.author: Arberry
16.text: We have set in heaven constellations and decked them out fair to the beholders,

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: And assuredly We have set constellations in the heaven and made it fairseeming unto the beholders

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: And indeed, We have put the big stars in the heaven and We beautified it for the beholders.

16.author: Itani
16.text: We placed constellations in the sky, and made them beautiful to the beholders.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: We have indeed set constellations in the heavens and have beautified them for the beholders,

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: And indeed, We have put the big stars in the heaven and We beautified it for the beholders.

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: And verily in the heaven we have set mansions of the stars, and We have beautified it for beholders.

16.author: Qarai
16.text: Certainly We have appointed houses in the sky and adorned them for the onlookers,

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: We have set constellations in the heavens and made them pleasing to the beholders,

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: And We have placed within the heaven great stars and have beautified it for the observers.

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: We have made constellations in the sky and decorated them for the onlookers.

16.author: Shakir
16.text: And certainly We have made strongholds in the heaven and We have made it fair seeming to the beholders.

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: We have placed constellations in heaven and have beautified it for beholders,

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: It is We Who have set out the zodiacal signs in the heavens, and made them fair-seeming to (all) beholders;

16.author: Transliteration
16.text: Walaqad jaAAalna fee alssamai buroojan wazayyannaha lilnnathireena

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: And We have preserved it from every accursed devil,

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: And We have kept it guarded from every outcast devil.

17.author: Arberry
17.text: and guarded them from every accursed Satan

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: And We have guarded it from every Satan damned.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: And We have guarded it (near heaven) from every outcast Shaitan (devil).

17.author: Itani
17.text: And We guarded them from every outcast devil.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: and have protected them against every accursed satan

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: And We have guarded it (near heaven) from every outcast Shaytan (devil).

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: And We have guarded it from every outcast devil,

17.author: Qarai
17.text: and We have guarded them from every outcast Satan,

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: and guarded them from every stoned satan.

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: And We have protected it from every devil expelled [from the mercy of Allah]

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: We have protected them from every condemned devil,

17.author: Shakir
17.text: And We guard it against every accursed Shaitan,

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: and We have guarded it from every accursed devil:

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: And (moreover) We have guarded them from every cursed devil:

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: Except the ones who listen on the sly, yet they are chased away by a shooting flame.

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: Except one who comes to eavesdrop – therefore a bright flame goes after him.

18.author: Arberry
18.text: excepting such as listens by stealth -- and he is pursued by a manifest flame.

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: Save him who stealeth the hearing, and him there followeth a flame gleaming.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: Except him (devil) that gains hearing by stealing, he is pursued by a clear flaming fire.

18.author: Itani
18.text: Except one who steals a hearing, and is followed by a visible projectile.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: save him who may eavesdrop, and then a bright flame pursues him.

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: Except him (devil) who steals the hearing, then he is pursued by a clear flaming fire.

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: Save him who stealeth the hearing, and them doth a clear flame pursue.

18.author: Qarai
18.text: except someone who may eavesdrop, whereat there pursues him a manifest flame.

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: Except for he who steals the listening and is then pursued by a visible flame.

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: Except one who steals a hearing and is pursued by a clear burning flame.

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: except for those who stealthily try to listen to the heavens, but who are chased away by a bright flame.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: But he who steals a hearing, so there follows him a visible flame.

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: but if anyone eavesdrops, he is pursued by a bright flaming fire.

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: But any that gains a hearing by stealth, is pursued by a flaming fire, bright (to see).

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Illa mani istaraqa alssamAAa faatbaAAahu shihabun mubeenun

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: We stretched the earth and placed upon it firm stabilisers, and made all things grow upon it balanced evenly.

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: And We spread out the earth, and placed mountains as anchors in it, and in it grew all things by a proper measure.

19.author: Arberry
19.text: And the earth -- We stretched it forth, and cast on it firm mountains, and We caused to grow therein of every thing justly weighed,

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: And the earth! We have stretched it out and have cast thereon mountains firm, and We have caused to spring up thereon everything weighed.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: And the earth We spread out, and placed therein firm mountains, and caused to grow therein all kinds of things in due proportion.

19.author: Itani
19.text: We spread the earth, and placed stabilizers in it, and in it We grew all things in proper measure.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: As for the earth, We have stretched it out and have cast on it firm mountains, and have caused to grow in it everything well-measured.

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: And We have spread out the earth, and have placed firm mountains in it, and caused all kinds of things to grow in it, in due proportion.

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: And the earth have We spread out, and placed therein firm hills, and caused each seemly thing to grow therein.

19.author: Qarai
19.text: And We spread out the earth, and cast in it firm mountains, and We grew in it every kind of balanced thing,

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: We have spread out the earth and set upon it firm mountains. Everything We have caused to grow therein is justly weighed;

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: And the earth - We have spread it and cast therein firmly set mountains and caused to grow therein [something] of every well-balanced thing.

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: We have spread out the earth, fixed mountains thereupon and caused everything to grow to its proper weight

19.author: Shakir
19.text: And the earth-- We have spread it forth and made in it firm mountains and caused to grow in it of every suitable thing.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: We have spread out the earth, and set upon it firm mountains and caused everything to grow in due proportion.

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: And the earth We have spread out (like a carpet); set thereon mountains firm and immovable; and produced therein all kinds of things in due balance.

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: Waalarda madadnaha waalqayna feeha rawasiya waanbatna feeha min kulli shayin mawzoonin

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: We have provided on it sustenance for you, and for those you cannot provide.

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: And created livelihoods for you in it, and created those for whom you do not provide the sustenance.

20.author: Arberry
20.text: and there appointed for you livelihood, and for those you provide not for.

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: And We have appointed thereon for you livelihoods and also for those of whom ye are not the providers.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: And We have provided therein means of living, for you and for those whom you provide not [moving (living) creatures, cattle, beasts, and other animals].

20.author: Itani
20.text: And in it We created livelihoods for you, and for those for whom you are not the providers.

20.author: Maududi
20.text: And We have provided sustenance for you on it and also for those of whom you are not the providers.

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: And in it We have provided means of living, for you and for those whom you provide not.

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: And we have given unto you livelihoods therein, and unto those for whom ye provide not.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: and made in it [various] means of livelihood for you and for those whom you do not provide for.

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: and there appointed for you is a livelihood, and for those you do not provide.

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: And We have made for you therein means of living and [for] those for whom you are not providers.

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: to provide you and those for whose sustenance you are not responsible, with the necessities of life.

20.author: Shakir
20.text: And We have made in it means of subsistence for you and for him for whom you are not the suppliers.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: We have provided therein a means of livelihood for you and for all those creatures for whom you do not provide.

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: And We have provided therein means of subsistence,- for you and for those for whose sustenance ye are not responsible.

20.author: Transliteration
20.text: WajaAAalna lakum feeha maAAayisha waman lastum lahu biraziqeena

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: Of all things there are We have the stores, and send them down in determined measure.

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: And there is not a thing the treasure* of which is not with Us; and We do not send it down except by a known measure. (* The power to create it.)

21.author: Arberry
21.text: Naught is there, but its treasuries are with Us, and We send it not down but in a known measure.

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: And there is not of aught but with Us are the treasures thereof, and We send it not down save in a measure known

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: And there is not a thing, but with Us are the stores thereof. And We send it not down except in a known measure.

21.author: Itani
21.text: There is not a thing but with Us are its stores, and We send it down only in precise measure.

21.author: Maududi
21.text: There is nothing except that its treasuries are with Us and We do not send it down except in a known measure.

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: And there is not a thing, but the supplies for it are with Us, and We do not send it down but in a known measure.

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: And there is not a thing but with Us are the stores thereof. And we send it not down save in appointed measure.

21.author: Qarai
21.text: There is not a thing but that its sources are with Us, and We do not send it down except in a known measure.

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: And there is not a thing but with Us are its treasuries, and We do not send it down except in a known measure.

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: And there is not a thing but that with Us are its depositories, and We do not send it down except according to a known measure.

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: With Us is the source of everything and We do not send it down except in a known quantity.

21.author: Shakir
21.text: And there is not a thing but with Us are the treasures of it, and We do not send it down but in a known measure.

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: There is not a thing but its storehouses are with Us. But We only send down each thing in an appropriate measure:

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: And there is not a thing but its (sources and) treasures (inexhaustible) are with Us; but We only send down thereof in due and ascertainable measures.

21.author: Transliteration
21.text: Wain min shayin illa AAindana khazainuhu wama nunazziluhu illa biqadarin maAAloomin

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: We send rain-impregnated winds, and water from the sky which you drink, but you are not the keepers of its store.

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: And We sent the winds that relieve the clouds’ burden, therefore caused water to descend from the sky, then give it to you to drink; and you are not at all its treasurers.

22.author: Arberry
22.text: And We loose the winds fertilising, and We send down out of heaven water, then We give it to you to drink, and you are not its treasurers.

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: And We send the winds fertilizing, then We send down water from the heaven and We give it to you to drink, and thereof ye could not be the treasurers.

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: And We send the winds fertilizing (to fill heavily the clouds with water), then caused the water (rain) to descend from the sky, and We gave it to you to drink, and it is not you who are the owners of its stores [i.e. to give water to whom you like or to withhold it from whom you like].

22.author: Itani
22.text: We send the fertilizing winds; and send down water from the sky, and give it to you to drink, and you are not the ones who store it.

22.author: Maududi
22.text: We send fertilizing winds, and then cause rain to descend from the sky, providing you abundant water to drink even though you could not have stored it up for yourselves.

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: And We send the winds fertilizing, then We cause the water to descend from the sky, and We give it to you to drink, and it is not you who are the owners of its supply.

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: And We send the winds fertilising, and cause water to descend from the sky, and give it you to drink. It is not ye who are the holders of the store thereof.

22.author: Qarai
22.text: And We send the fertilizing winds and send down water from the sky providing it for you to drink and you are not maintainers of its resources.

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: We send the winds fertilizing, and We send down out of heaven water, from which you drink and you are not its treasurers.

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: And We have sent the fertilizing winds and sent down water from the sky and given you drink from it. And you are not its retainers.

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: We send impregnating winds and send down water from the sky for you to drink and you have no (control over its) storage.

22.author: Shakir
22.text: And We send the winds fertilizing, then send down water from the cloud so We give it to you to drink of, nor is it you who store it up.

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: We let loose fertilizing winds, and bring water from the sky for you to drink; and you could not have stored it up for yourselves.

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: And We send the fecundating winds, then cause the rain to descend from the sky, therewith providing you with water (in abundance), though ye are not the guardians of its stores.

22.author: Transliteration
22.text: Waarsalna alrriyaha lawaqiha faanzalna mina alssamai maan faasqaynakumoohu wama antum lahu bikhazineena

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: It is We who give life and We who give death, and We are the One who will abide.

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: And indeed it is We Who give life and it is We Who cause death, and We are the Inheritors.

23.author: Arberry
23.text: It is We who give life, and make to die, and it is We who are the inheritors.

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: And verily We! it is We who give life and death, and We shall be the survivors.

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: And certainly We! We it is Who give life, and cause death, and We are the Inheritors.

23.author: Itani
23.text: It is We who give life and cause death, and We are the Inheritors.

23.author: Maududi
23.text: It is indeed We Who grant life and cause death and it is We who shall be the sole Inheritors of all.

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: And certainly We! It is We Who give life, and cause death, and We are the Inheritors.

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: Lo! and it is We, even We, Who quicken and give death, and We are the Inheritor.

23.author: Qarai
23.text: Indeed it is We who give life and bring death and We are the inheritors.

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: And surely, it is We who give life and make to die. We are the Inheritor.

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: And indeed, it is We who give life and cause death, and We are the Inheritor.

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: It is We who give life and cause things to die and We are the sole Heirs.

23.author: Shakir
23.text: And most surely We bring to life and cause to die and We are the heirs.

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: Truly, it is We who bring to life and We who cause death and We are the inheritor of all things.

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: And verily, it is We Who give life, and Who give death: it is We Who remain inheritors (after all else passes away).

23.author: Transliteration
23.text: Wainna lanahnu nuhyee wanumeetu wanahnu alwarithoona

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: We surely know those of you who go forward and those of you who lag behind.

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: And indeed We know those among you who came forward and indeed We know those among you who remained behind.

24.author: Arberry
24.text: We know the ones of you who press forward, and We know the laggards;

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: And assuredly We know those of you who have gone before and those who will come hereafter.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: And indeed, We know the first generations of you who had passed away, and indeed, We know the present generations of you (mankind), and also those who will come afterwards.

24.author: Itani
24.text: And We know those of you who go forward, and We know those who lag behind.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: Surely We know those of you who have passed before and those who will come later.

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: And indeed, We know the first generations of you who have passed away, and indeed, We know the present generations of you (mankind), and also those who will come afterwards.

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: And verily We know the eager among you and verily We know the laggards.

24.author: Qarai
24.text: Certainly We know the predecessors among you and certainly We know the successors,

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: We know those of you who press forward, and We know the laggards,

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: And We have already known the preceding [generations] among you, and We have already known the later [ones to come].

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: We know the people who lived before you and those who will come into existence after you.

24.author: Shakir
24.text: And certainly We know those of you who have gone before and We certainly know those who shall come later.

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: We know those who lived before you and those who will come after you.

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: To Us are known those of you who hasten forward, and those who lag behind.

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Walaqad AAalimna almustaqdimeena minkum walaqad AAalimna almustakhireena

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: Your Lord will surely gather them together: Certainly He is all-wise and all-knowing.

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: Indeed your Lord only will raise them all on the Day of Resurrection; indeed only He is Wise, All Knowing.

25.author: Arberry
25.text: and it is thy Lord shall muster them, and He is All-wise, All-knowing.

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: And veriiy thy Lord! He will gather them, and verily He is Wise. Knowing.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: And verily, your Lord will gather them together. Truly, He is All-Wise, All-Knowing.

25.author: Itani
25.text: It is your Lord who will gather them together. He is the Wise, the Knowing.

25.author: Maududi
25.text: Indeed your Lord will gather them all together. Surely He is All-Wise, All-Knowing.

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: And verily, your Lord will gather them together. Truly, He is Most Wise, (and) Knowing.

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: Lo! thy Lord will gather them together. Lo! He is Wise, Aware.

25.author: Qarai
25.text: and indeed it is your Lord who will resurrect them. Indeed, He is all-wise, all-knowing.

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: and it is your Lord who will gather them. He is Wise, Knowing.

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: And indeed, your Lord will gather them; indeed, He is Wise and Knowing.

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: Your Lord will resurrect them all; He is All-wise and All-knowing.

25.author: Shakir
25.text: And surely your Lord will gather them together; surely He is Wise, Knowing.

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: It is your Lord who will gather them. He is all wise and all knowing.

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: Assuredly it is thy Lord Who will gather them together: for He is perfect in Wisdom and Knowledge.

25.author: Transliteration
25.text: Wainna rabbaka huwa yahshuruhum innahu hakeemun AAaleemun

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: Man We fashioned from fermented clay dried tingling hard,

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: Indeed We created man from sounding clay made out of black smelly mud.

26.author: Arberry
26.text: Surely We created man of a clay of mud moulded,

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: And assuredly We have created human being from ringing clay of loam moulded.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: And indeed, We created man from sounding clay of altered black smooth mud.

26.author: Itani
26.text: We created the human being from clay, from molded mud.

26.author: Maududi
26.text: Surely We brought man into being out of dry ringing clay which was wrought from black mud,

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: And indeed, We created man from dried (sounding) clay of altered mud.

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: Verily We created man of potter's clay of black mud altered,

26.author: Qarai
26.text: Certainly We created man out of a dry clay [drawn] from an aging mud,

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: We created mankind from clay, molded from mud,

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: And We did certainly create man out of clay from an altered black mud.

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: We have created the human being out of pure mud-moulded clay

26.author: Shakir
26.text: And certainly We created man of clay that gives forth sound, of black mud fashioned in shape.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: We created man out of dry clay, from moulded mud,

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: We created man from sounding clay, from mud moulded into shape;

26.author: Transliteration
26.text: Walaqad khalaqna alinsana min salsalin min hamain masnoonin

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: As We fashioned jinns before from intense radiated heat.

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: And created the jinn before him, from smokeless fire.

27.author: Arberry
27.text: and the jinn created We before of fire flaming.

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: And the Jinn, We had created them afore of the fire of the scroching wind.

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: And the jinn, We created aforetime from the smokeless flame of fire.

27.author: Itani
27.text: And the jinn We created before, from piercing fire.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: while We had brought the jinn into being before out of blazing fire.

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: And the Jinn, We created earlier from the smokeless flame of fire.

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: And the jinn did We create aforetime of essential fire.

27.author: Qarai
27.text: and We created the jinn earlier, out of a piercing fire.

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: and before him We created the jinn from smokeless fire.

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: And the jinn We created before from scorching fire.

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: and the jinn before (the human being) of smokeless fire.

27.author: Shakir
27.text: And the jinn We created before, of intensely hot fire.

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: and the jinn We had created before from flaming fire.

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: And the Jinn race, We had created before, from the fire of a scorching wind.

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Waaljanna khalaqnahu min qablu min nari alssamoomi

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: But when your Lord said to the angels: "I am verily going to create a human being from fermented clay dried tingling hard;

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: And recall when your Lord said to the angels, “I will create man from sounding clay made out of black smelly mud.”

28.author: Arberry
28.text: And when thy Lord said to the angels, 'See, I am creating a mortal of a clay of mud moulded.

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: And recall what time thy Lord said unto the angels: verily I am about to createa man from ringing clay of loam moulded.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: And (remember) when your Lord said to the angels: "I am going to create a man (Adam) from sounding clay of altered black smooth mud.

28.author: Itani
28.text: Your Lord said to the angels, “I am creating a human being from clay, from molded mud.”

28.author: Maududi
28.text: Recall when your Lord said to the angels: "I will indeed bring into being a human being out of dry ringing clay wrought from black mud.

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: And (remember) when your Lord said to the angels: "I am going to create a man (Adam) from dried (sounding) clay of altered mud. "

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: And (remember) when thy Lord said unto the angels: Lo! I am creating a mortal out of potter's clay of black mud altered,

28.author: Qarai
28.text: When your Lord said to the angels, ‘Indeed I am going to create a human out of a dry clay [drawn] from an aging mud.

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: When your Lord said to the angels: 'See, I am creating a mortal from clay of molded mud.

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: And [mention, O Muhammad], when your Lord said to the angels, "I will create a human being out of clay from an altered black mud.

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: When your Lord said to the angels, "I will create the human being out of pure mud-moulded clay.

28.author: Shakir
28.text: And when your Lord said to the angels: Surely I am going to create a mortal of the essence of black mud fashioned in shape.

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: Your Lord said to the angels, "I am about to bring into being a man wrought from mud.

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: Behold! thy Lord said to the angels: "I am about to create man, from sounding clay from mud moulded into shape;

28.author: Transliteration
28.text: Waith qala rabbuka lilmalaikati innee khaliqun basharan min salsalin min hamain masnoonin

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: And when I have fashioned him and breathed into him of My spirit, bow before him in homage;"

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: “Therefore when I have properly fashioned him and breathed into him a chosen noble soul from Myself, fall down before him in prostration.”

29.author: Arberry
29.text: When I have shaped him, and breathed My spirit in him, fall you down, bowing before him!'

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: Then when I have formed him and breathed into him of My Spirit fall down unto him prostrate.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: "So, when I have fashioned him completely and breathed into him (Adam) the soul which I created for him, then fall (you) down prostrating yourselves unto him."

29.author: Itani
29.text: “When I have formed him, and breathed into him of My spirit, fall down prostrating before him.”

29.author: Maududi
29.text: When I have completed shaping him and have breathed into him of My Spirit, then fall you down before him in prostration."

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: "So, when I have fashioned him completely and breathed into him (Adam) of My spirit (the soul which I created for him,) then fall down, prostrating yourselves before him."

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: So, when I have made him and have breathed into him of My Spirit, do ye fall down, prostrating yourselves unto him.

29.author: Qarai
29.text: So when I have proportioned him and breathed into him of My spirit, then fall down in prostration before him.’

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: When I have shaped him and ran My created soul in him fall down prostrating towards him'.

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: And when I have proportioned him and breathed into him of My [created] soul, then fall down to him in prostration."

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: When it is properly shaped and I have blown My Spirit into it, you should then bow down in prostration".

29.author: Shakir
29.text: So when I have made him complete and breathed into him of My spirit, fall down making obeisance to him.

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: When I have formed him and breathed My spirit into him, fall down in prostration before him,"

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: "When I have fashioned him (in due proportion) and breathed into him of My spirit, fall ye down in obeisance unto him."

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Faitha sawwaytuhu wanafakhtu feehi min roohee faqaAAoo lahu sajideena

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: The angels bowed in homage in a body

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: Therefore all the angels, each and every one of them, fell prostrate.

30.author: Arberry
30.text: Then the angels bowed themselves all together,

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: So the angels prostrated themselves, all of them together.

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: So, the angels prostrated themselves, all of them together.

30.author: Itani
30.text: So the angels prostrated themselves, all together.

30.author: Maududi
30.text: So, the angels - all of them - fell down in prostration,

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: So the angels prostrated themselves, all of them together.

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: So the angels fell prostrate, all of them together

30.author: Qarai
30.text: Thereat the angels prostrated, all of them together,

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: So the angels prostrated - all of them entirely,

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: All the angels prostrated before Adam

30.author: Shakir
30.text: So the angels made obeisance, all of them together,

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: then the angels all prostrated themselves together.

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: So the angels prostrated themselves, all of them together:

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: Except Iblis. He refused to bow with the adorers.

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: Except Iblis (Satan); he refused to be among those who prostrated.

31.author: Arberry
31.text: save Iblis; he refused to be among those bowing.

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: But Iblis did not; he refused to be with the prostrates.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: Except Iblis (Satan), - he refused to be among the prostrators.

31.author: Itani
31.text: Except for Satan. He refused to be among those who prostrated themselves.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: except Iblis; he refused to join those who prostrated.

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: Except Iblis (Shaytan) - he refused to be among the prostrate.

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: Save Iblis. He refused to be among the prostrate.

31.author: Qarai
31.text: but not Iblis: he refused to be among those who prostrated.

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: except iblis (father of the jinn) who refused to be one of those who prostrated themselves.

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: Except Iblees, he refused to be with those who prostrated.

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: except Iblis who refused to join with the others in prostration.

31.author: Shakir
31.text: But Iblis (did it not); he refused to be with those who made obeisance.

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: But Satan did not; he refused to join those who prostrated themselves.

31.author: Yusuf Ali

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: Illa ibleesa aba an yakoona maAAa alssajideena

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: "How is it, O Iblis," said (the Lord), "you did not join those who bowed in homage?"

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: Said Allah, “O Iblis! What happened to you that you stayed apart from those who prostrated?”

32.author: Arberry
32.text: Said He, 'What ails thee, Iblis, that thou art not among those bowing?'

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: Allah said: O Iblis! what aileth thou that thou art not with the prostrates?

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: (Allah) said: "O Iblis (Satan)! What is your reason for not being among the prostrators?"

32.author: Itani
32.text: He said, “O Satan, what kept you from being among those who prostrated themselves?”

32.author: Maududi
32.text: The Lord inquired: "Iblis! What is the matter with you that you did not join those who prostrated?"

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: (Allah) said: "O Iblis! What is your reason for not being among the prostrate"

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: He said: O Iblis! What aileth thee that thou art not among the prostrate?

32.author: Qarai
32.text: He said, ‘O Iblis! What kept you from being among those who have prostrated?’

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: He said: 'iblis, what is the matter with you, that you do you not prostrate yourself'

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: [Allah] said, O Iblees, what is [the matter] with you that you are not with those who prostrate?"

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: God asked Iblis, "What made you not join the others in prostration?"

32.author: Shakir
32.text: He said: O Iblis! what excuse have you that you are not with those who make obeisance?

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: God asked him, "What is the matter with you, that you are not among those who have prostrated themselves?"

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: (Allah) said: "O Iblis! what is your reason for not being among those who prostrated themselves?"

32.author: Transliteration
32.text: Qala ya ibleesu ma laka alla takoona maAAa alssajideena

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: "How could I bow," said he, "before a mortal whom You created from fermented clay dried tingling hard?"

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: He answered, “It does not befit me to prostrate myself to a human whom You have created from sounding clay made out of black smelly mud.”

33.author: Arberry
33.text: Said he, 'I would never bow myself before a mortal whom Thou hast created of a clay of mud moulded.'

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: He said it was not for me that should prostrate myself unto a human being whom Thou hast created from ringing clay of loam moulded.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: [Iblis (Satan)] said: "I am not the one to prostrate myself to a human being, whom You created from sounding clay of altered black smooth mud."

33.author: Itani
33.text: He said, “I am not about to prostrate myself before a human being, whom You created from clay, from molded mud.”

33.author: Maududi
33.text: He said: "It does not behove of me to prostrate myself before a human being whom You have created out of dry ringing clay wrought from black mud."

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: [Iblis] said: "I am not one to prostrate myself to a human, whom You created from dried (sounding) clay of altered mud."

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: He said: I am not one to prostrate myself unto a mortal whom Thou hast created out of potter's clay of black mud altered!

33.author: Qarai
33.text: Said he, ‘I will not prostrate before a human whom You have created out of a dry clay [drawn] from an aging mud.’

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: He replied: 'I will not prostrate to a mortal You have created of clay, from molded mud'

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: He said, "Never would I prostrate to a human whom You created out of clay from an altered black mud."

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: Iblis replied, "I did not want to prostrate before a human being whom You have created out of mud-moulded clay".

33.author: Shakir
33.text: He said: I am not such that I should make obeisance to a mortal whom Thou hast created of the essence of black mud fashioned in shape.

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: He replied, "I am not one to prostrate myself to a man whom You have created out of a clay of moulded mud."

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: (Iblis) said: "I am not one to prostrate myself to man, whom Thou didst create from sounding clay, from mud moulded into shape."

33.author: Transliteration
33.text: Qala lam akun liasjuda libasharin khalaqtahu min salsalin min hamain masnoonin

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: "Go hence, execrable," (said the Lord), "from this place,

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: Said Allah, “Therefore exit from Paradise, for you are an outcast.”

34.author: Arberry
34.text: Said He, 'Then go thou forth hence; thou art accursed.

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: Allah said: then get thee forth therefrom; verily thou art one damned.

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: (Allah) said: "Then, get out from here, for verily, you are Rajim (an outcast or a cursed one)." [Tafsir At-Tabari]

34.author: Itani
34.text: He said, “Then get out of here, for you are an outcast”.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: The Lord said: "Then get out of here; you are rejected,

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: (Allah) said: "Then leave, for verily, you are an outcast. "

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: He said: Then go thou forth from hence, for lo! thou art outcast.

34.author: Qarai
34.text: He said, ‘Begone hence, for you are indeed an outcast,

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: [Allah] said, "Then get out of it, for indeed, you are expelled.

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: God told him, "Get out of the garden; you are rejected

34.author: Shakir
34.text: He said: Then get out of it, for surely you are driven away:

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: God said, "Then get out of here; for you are accursed,

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: (Allah) said: "Then get thee out from here; for thou art rejected, accursed.

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: Condemned till the day of Doom!"

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: “And indeed you are accursed till the Day of Judgement.”

35.author: Arberry
35.text: Upon thee shall rest the curse, till the Day of Doom.'

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: And verily on thee shall be the curse till the Day of Requital.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: "And verily, the curse shall be upon you till the Day of Recompense (i.e. the Day of Resurrection)."

35.author: Itani
35.text: “And the curse will be upon you until the Day of Judgment.”

35.author: Maududi
35.text: and there shall be a curse upon you till the Day of Recompense."

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: "And verily, the curse shall be upon you until the Day of Recompense."

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: And lo! the curse shall be upon thee till the Day of Judgment.

35.author: Qarai
35.text: and indeed the curse shall lie on you until the Day of Retribution.’

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: A curse shall be on you till the Day of Recompense'

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: And indeed, upon you is the curse until the Day of Recompense."

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: and will be subjected to condemnation until the Day of Judgment."

35.author: Shakir
35.text: And surely on you is curse until the day of judgment.

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: and the curse shall be on you till the Day of Judgement!"

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: "And the curse shall be on thee till the day of Judgment."

35.author: Transliteration
35.text: Wainna AAalayka allaAAnata ila yawmi alddeeni

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: "O my Lord," said he, "give me respite till the day the dead are raised."

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: He said, “My Lord! Grant me respite till the day when they will all be raised.”

36.author: Arberry
36.text: Said he, 'My Lord, respite me till the day they shall be raised.'

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: He said: my Lord! respite me then till the Day whereon they will be raised up.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: [Iblis (Satan)] said: "O my Lord! Give me then respite till the Day they (the dead) will be resurrected."

36.author: Itani
36.text: He said, “My Lord, reprieve me until the Day they are resurrected.”

36.author: Maududi
36.text: Iblis said: "My Lord! Grant me respite till the Day when they will be resurrected."

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: [Iblis] said: "O my Lord! Then give me respite until the Day they (the dead) will be resurrected."

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: He said: My Lord! Reprieve me till the day when they are raised.

36.author: Qarai
36.text: He said, ‘My Lord! Respite me till the day they will be resurrected.’

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: He said: 'My Lord, reprieve me till the Day they are raised'

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: He said, "My Lord, then reprieve me until the Day they are resurrected."

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: Iblis prayed, "Lord, grant me respite until the Day of Judgment".

36.author: Shakir
36.text: He said: My Lord! then respite me till the time when they are raised.

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: Satan said, "O my Lord! Grant me respite till the Day of Resurrection."

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: (Iblis) said: "O my Lord! give me then respite till the Day the (dead) are raised."

36.author: Transliteration
36.text: Qala rabbi faanthirnee ila yawmi yubAAathoona

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: "You are among the reprieved," (said the Lord),

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: Said Allah, “You are of those given respite.”

37.author: Arberry
37.text: Said He, 'Thou art among the ones that are respited

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: Allah said: verily then thou art of the respited:

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: Allah said: "Then, verily, you are of those reprieved,

37.author: Itani
37.text: He said, “You are of those reprieved.”

37.author: Maududi
37.text: Allah said: "For sure you are granted respite

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: Allah said: "Then verily, you are of those reprieved,"

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: He said: Then lo! thou art of those reprieved

37.author: Qarai
37.text: Said He, ‘You are indeed among the reprieved

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: He answered: 'You are among those reprieved

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: [Allah] said, "So indeed, you are of those reprieved

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: The Lord said, "Your request is granted

37.author: Shakir
37.text: He said: So surely you are of the respited ones

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: “Till the Day of a well-known time.”

38.author: Arberry
38.text: unto the day of a known time.'

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: Till the Day of the Time known.

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: "Till the Day of the time appointed."

38.author: Itani
38.text: “Until the Day of the time appointed.”

38.author: Maududi
38.text: until the day of a known time."

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: "Until the Day of the time appointed."

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: Till the Day of appointed time.

38.author: Qarai
38.text: until the day of the known time.’

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: Until the Day of the time well-known."

38.author: Shakir
38.text: Till the period of the time made known.

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: "Till the Day of the Time appointed."

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: "O my Lord," he said, "since You have led me into error I'll beguile them with the pleasures of the world and lead them astray,

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: He said, “My Lord! I swear by the fact that You sent me astray, I shall distract them in the earth, and I shall lead all of them astray.”

39.author: Arberry
39.text: Said he, 'My Lord, for Thy perverting me I shall deck all fair to them in the earth, and I shall pervert them, all together,

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: He said: my Lord! because Thou hast led me to err I will surely make things fairseeming unto them on the earth and will surely seduce them all:

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: [Iblis (Satan)] said: "O my Lord! Because you misled me, I shall indeed adorn the path of error for them (mankind) on the earth, and I shall mislead them all.

39.author: Itani
39.text: He said, “My Lord, since You have lured me away, I will glamorize for them on earth, and I will lure them all away.”

39.author: Maududi
39.text: Iblis said: "My Lord! In the manner You led me to error, I will make things on earth seem attractive to them and lead all of them to error,

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: [Iblis] said: "O my Lord! Because You misled me, I shall indeed adorn the path of error for them (mankind) on the earth, and I shall mislead them all."

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: He said: My Lord! Because Thou hast sent me astray, I verily shall adorn the path of error for them in the earth, and shall mislead them every one,

39.author: Qarai
39.text: He said, ‘My Lord! As You have consigned me to perversity, I will surely glamorize [evil] for them on the earth, and I will surely pervert them, all

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: (satan) said: 'My Lord, for Your perverting me, I shall make (matters) in the earth seem most fair to them and I shall pervert all,

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: [Iblees] said, "My Lord, because You have put me in error, I will surely make [disobedience] attractive to them on earth, and I will mislead them all

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: Iblis said, "Lord, because you have caused me to go astray, I shall make earthly things attractive to (people) and mislead all of them

39.author: Shakir
39.text: He said: My Lord! because Thou hast made life evil to me, I will certainly make (evil) fair-seeming to them on earth, and I will certainly cause them all to deviate

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: He said, "My Lord, since You have let me go astray. I shall make the path of error seem alluring to them on the earth and shall mislead them all,

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: (Iblis) said: "O my Lord! because Thou hast put me in the wrong, I will make (wrong) fair-seeming to them on the earth, and I will put them all in the wrong,-
