39.text: Qala rabbi bima aghwaytanee laozayyinanna lahum fee alardi walaoghwiyannahum ajmaAAeena

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: Except the chosen ones among Your creatures."

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: “Except those among them who are Your chosen bondmen.”

40.author: Arberry
40.text: excepting those Thy servants among them that are devoted.

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: But not such of them as are Thy bondmen single-hearted.

40.author: Itani
40.text: “Except for Your sincere servants among them.”

40.author: Maududi
40.text: except those of Your servants whom You have singled out for Yourself."

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: Save such of them as are Thy perfectly devoted slaves.

40.author: Qarai
40.text: except Your dedicated servants among them.’

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: except the devoted amongst Your worshippers.

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: Except, among them, Your chosen servants."

40.author: Shakir
40.text: Except Thy servants from among them, the devoted ones.

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: "Except Thy servants among them, sincere and purified (by Thy Grace)."

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: (To which God) said: "This way is right by Me.

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: Said Allah, “This path* leads straight towards Me.” (* The path of the chosen bondmen.)

41.author: Arberry
41.text: Said He, 'This is for Me a straight path:

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: Allah said: this is the path leeding unto Me straight.

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: (Allah) said: "This is the Way which will lead straight to Me."

41.author: Itani
41.text: He said, “This is a right way with Me.”

41.author: Maududi
41.text: Allah said: "Here is the path that leads straight to Me.

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: (Allah) said: "This is the way which will lead straight to Me."

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: He said: This is a right course incumbent upon Me:

41.author: Qarai
41.text: He said, ‘This is the path [leading] straight to Me.

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: He (Allah) said: 'This is for Me the Right Path

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: [Allah] said, "This is a path [of return] to Me [that is] straight.

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: God said, "The path which leads to Me is a straight

41.author: Shakir
41.text: He said: This is a right way with Me:

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: God said, "This is the path which leads straight to Me.

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: (Allah) said: "This (way of My sincere servants) is indeed a way that leads straight to Me.

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: No power shall you have over (all) My creatures except those who fall into error and follow you,

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: “Indeed you do not have any power over My bondmen, except those wanderers who follow you.” (The devil is unsuccessful in tempting Allah’s chosen bondmen to commit sin.)

42.author: Arberry
42.text: over My servants thou shalt have no authority, except those that follow thee,

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: Verily as for My bondmen, no authority shalt thou have over them, except the erring one who follow thee.

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: "Certainly, you shall have no authority over My slaves, except those who follow you of the Ghawin (Mushrikun and those who go astray, criminals, polytheists, and evil-doers, etc.).

42.author: Itani
42.text: “Over My servants you have no authority, except for the sinners who follow you.”

42.author: Maududi
42.text: Over My true servants you will be able to exercise no power, your power will be confined to the erring ones, those who choose to follow you.

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: "Certainly, you shall have no authority over My servants, except those of the astray who follow you."

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: Lo! as for My slaves, thou hast no power over any of them save such of the froward as follow thee,

42.author: Qarai
42.text: Indeed, as for My servants you do not have any authority over them, except the perverse who follow you,

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: over My worshipers you have no authority, except the perverse that follow you.

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: Indeed, My servants - no authority will you have over them, except those who follow you of the deviators.

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: and you have no authority over My servants except the erring ones who follow you.

42.author: Shakir
42.text: Surely. as regards My servants, you have no authority, over them except those who follow you of the deviators.

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: Surely, you shall have no power over My true servants, except those misguided ones who choose to follow you.

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: "For over My servants no authority shalt thou have, except such as put themselves in the wrong and follow thee."

42.author: Transliteration
42.text: Inna AAibadee laysa laka AAalayhim sultanun illa mani ittabaAAaka mina alghaweena

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: For whom the ordained place is surely Hell,

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: And indeed hell is the promise for all of them.

43.author: Arberry
43.text: being perverse; Gehenna shall be their promised land all together.

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: And verily Hell is the place promised unto them all.

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: "And surely, Hell is the promised place for them all.

43.author: Itani
43.text: And Hell is the meeting-place for them all.

43.author: Maududi
43.text: Surely Hell is the promised place for all of them."

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: And surely, Hell is the place promised for them all.

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: And lo! for all such, hell will be the promised place.

43.author: Qarai
43.text: and indeed hell is the tryst of them all.

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: Gehenna (Hell) will be the promise for all of them.

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: And indeed, Hell is the promised place for them all.

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: Hell is the promised place for them all.

43.author: Shakir
43.text: And surely Hell is the promised place of them all:

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: Surely, Hell is the place to which they are destined,

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: And verily, Hell is the promised abode for them all!

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: Which has several gates, and each gate is marked for every section of them."

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: It has seven gates; for each gate is a portion assigned from them.

44.author: Arberry
44.text: Seven gates it has, and unto each gate a set portion of them belongs.'

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: Unto it are seven portals; unto each portal is a Portion of them assigned.

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: "It (Hell) has seven gates, for each of those gates is a (special) class (of sinners) assigned.

44.author: Itani
44.text: “It has seven doors; for each door is an assigned class.”

44.author: Maududi
44.text: There are seven gates in it, and to each gate a portion of them has been allotted.

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: It has seven gates, for each of those gates is a class assigned.

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: It hath seven gates, and each gate hath an appointed portion.

44.author: Qarai
44.text: It has seven gates, and to each gate belongs a separate portion of them.’

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: It has seven gates, and through each gate a portion of them belong.

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: It has seven gates; for every gate is of them a portion designated."

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: It has seven gates and each gate is assigned for a certain group of people.

44.author: Shakir
44.text: It has seven gates; for every gate there shall be a separate party of them.

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: it has seven gates: and each gate has a portion of them allotted to it.

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: To it are seven gates: for each of those gates is a (special) class (of sinners) assigned.

44.author: Transliteration
44.text: Laha sabAAatu abwabin likulli babin minhum juzon maqsoomun

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: Verily those who keep away from evil and follow the straight path shall be in the midst of gardens and springs of water.

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: Indeed the pious are amidst Gardens and springs.

45.author: Arberry
45.text: But the godfearing shall be amidst gardens and fountains:

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: Verily the God-fearing shall be amidst gardens and springs.

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: "Truly! The Muttaqun (pious and righteous persons - see V. 2:2) will be amidst Gardens and water-springs (Paradise).

45.author: Itani
45.text: But the righteous will be in gardens with springs.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: As for the God-fearing, they shall be amid gardens and springs.

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: Truly, those who have Taqwa, will dwell in Gardens and water springs.

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: Lo! those who ward off (evil) are among gardens and watersprings.

45.author: Qarai
45.text: Indeed the Godwary will be amid gardens and springs.

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: But the cautious shall live amongst gardens and fountains:

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: Indeed, the righteous will be within gardens and springs.

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: The pious will live in gardens with streams

45.author: Shakir
45.text: Surely those who guard (against evil) shall be in the midst of gardens and fountains:

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: Truly, the God-fearing shall dwell amid gardens and fountains --

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: The righteous (will be) amid gardens and fountains (of clear-flowing water).

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: "Enter in peace and tranquility," (they will be told).

46.author: Arberry
46.text: 'Enter you them, in peace and security!'

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: "(It will be said to them): 'Enter therein (Paradise), in peace and security.'

46.author: Maududi
46.text: They will be told: "Enter it in peace and security."

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: (It will be said to them): "Enter it in peace and security."

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: (And it is said unto them): Enter them in peace, secure.

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: [Having been told], "Enter it in peace, safe [and secure]."

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: and they will be told to enter there in peace and safety.

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: (Their greeting will be): "Enter ye here in peace and security."

47.author: Ahmed Ali
47.text: We shall cast out any grudge they may have in their hearts. (There) they will sit on couches face to face like brothers together.

47.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
47.text: And We have removed any resentments which were in their breasts – they are brothers, sitting face to face on thrones.

47.author: Arberry
47.text: We shall strip away all rancour that is in their breasts; as brothers they shall be upon couches set face to face;

47.author: Daryabadi
47.text: And We shall have removed whatsoever of grudge may be in their breasts: brethren they. sitting upon couches facing each other.

47.author: Hilali & Khan
47.text: "And We shall remove from their breasts any sense of injury (that they may have), (So they will be like) brothers facing each other on thrones.

47.author: Itani
47.text: And We will remove all ill-feelings from their hearts—brothers and sisters, on couches facing one another.

47.author: Maududi
47.text: And We shall purge their breasts of all traces of rancour; and they shall be seated on couches facing one another as brothers.

47.author: Mubarakpuri
47.text: And We shall remove any deep feeling of bitterness from their breasts. (So they will be like) brothers facing each other on thrones.

47.author: Pickthall
47.text: And We remove whatever rancour may be in their breasts. As brethren, face to face, (they rest) on couches raised.

47.author: Qarai
47.text: We will remove whatever rancour there is in their breasts; [intimate like] brothers, [they will be reclining] on couches, facing one another.

47.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
47.text: We shall remove all rancor from their hearts, and as brethren they shall recline on couches face to face.

47.author: Saheeh International
47.text: And We will remove whatever is in their breasts of resentment, [so they will be] brothers, on thrones facing each other.

47.author: Sarwar
47.text: We shall remove all hatred from their breasts and make them as brothers reclining on thrones facing one another.

47.author: Shakir
47.text: And We will root out whatever of rancor is in their breasts-- (they shall be) as brethren, on raised couches, face to face.

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: We shall cleanse their hearts of all traces of ill-will; they will be like brethren seated on couches facing one another.

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: And We shall remove from their hearts any lurking sense of injury: (they will be) brothers (joyfully) facing each other on thrones (of dignity).

47.author: Transliteration
47.text: WanazaAAna ma fee sudoorihim min ghillin ikhwanan AAala sururin mutaqabileena

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: No weariness will come upon them, nor will they be sent away from there.

48.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
48.text: Neither any hardship is to reach them in it, nor are they to be expelled from it.

48.author: Arberry
48.text: no fatigue there shall smite them, neither shall they ever be driven forth from there.

48.author: Daryabadi
48.text: There shall touch them no toil therein, nor therefrom they shall ever be driven out.

48.author: Hilali & Khan
48.text: "No sense of fatigue shall touch them, nor shall they (ever) be asked to leave it."

48.author: Itani
48.text: No fatigue will ever touch them therein, nor will they be asked to leave it.

48.author: Maududi
48.text: They shall face no fatigue in it, nor shall they ever be driven out of it.

48.author: Mubarakpuri
48.text: No sense of fatigue shall touch them, nor shall they be asked to leave it.

48.author: Pickthall
48.text: Toil cometh not unto them there, nor will they be expelled from thence.

48.author: Qarai
48.text: Therein neither weariness shall touch them, nor will they [ever] be expelled from it.

48.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
48.text: There, no fatigue shall smite them, nor shall they ever be driven from it'

48.author: Saheeh International
48.text: No fatigue will touch them therein, nor from it will they [ever] be removed.

48.author: Sarwar
48.text: No fatigue will touch them nor will they be expelled therefrom."

48.author: Shakir
48.text: Toil shall not afflict them in it, nor shall they be ever ejected from it.

48.author: Wahiduddin Khan
48.text: They will not be affected by any weariness there, and they will never be made to leave."

48.author: Yusuf Ali
48.text: There no sense of fatigue shall touch them, nor shall they (ever) be asked to leave.

48.author: Transliteration
48.text: La yamassuhum feeha nasabun wama hum minha bimukhrajeena

49.author: Ahmed Ali
49.text: Announce to My creatures that I am indeed forgiving and kind,

49.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
49.text: Inform My bondmen that undoubtedly, I surely am the Oft-Forgiving, the Most Merciful.

49.author: Arberry
49.text: Tell My servants I am the All-forgiving, the All-compassionate,

49.author: Daryabadi
49.text: Declare thou unto My bondmen: verily I! I am the Forgiver, the Merciful.

49.author: Hilali & Khan
49.text: Declare (O Muhammad SAW) unto My slaves, that truly, I am the Oft-Forgiving, the Most-Merciful.

49.author: Itani
49.text: Inform My servants that I am the Forgiver, the Merciful.

49.author: Maududi
49.text: (O Prophet), declare to My servants that I am indeed Ever Forgiving, Most Merciful.

49.author: Mubarakpuri
49.text: Declare to My servants that I am truly the Forgiving, the Most Merciful.

49.author: Pickthall
49.text: Announce, (O Muhammad) unto My slaves that verily I am the Forgiving, the Merciful,

49.author: Qarai
49.text: Inform my servants that I am indeed the All-forgiving, the All-merciful,

49.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
49.text: Tell My worshipers that I am the Forgiving, the Most Merciful,

49.author: Saheeh International
49.text: [O Muhammad], inform My servants that it is I who am the Forgiving, the Merciful.

49.author: Sarwar
49.text: (Muhammad), tell My servants that I am All-forgiving and All-merciful

49.author: Shakir
49.text: Inform My servants that I am the Forgiving, the Merciful,

49.author: Wahiduddin Khan
49.text: Tell My servants that I alone am the Forgiving, the Merciful One,

49.author: Yusuf Ali
49.text: Tell My servants that I am indeed the Oft-forgiving, Most Merciful;

49.author: Transliteration
49.text: Nabbi AAibadee annee ana alghafooru alrraheemu

50.author: Ahmed Ali
50.text: But My punishment is surely a painful one.

50.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
50.text: And that indeed the punishment of Mine is a painful punishment.

50.author: Arberry
50.text: and that My chastisement is the painful chastisement.

50.author: Daryabadi
50.text: And verily My torment! that is the torment afflictive

50.author: Hilali & Khan
50.text: And that My Torment is indeed the most painful torment.

50.author: Itani
50.text: And that My punishment is the painful punishment.

50.author: Maududi
50.text: At the same time, My chastisement is highly painful.

50.author: Mubarakpuri
50.text: And that My torment is indeed the most painful torment.

50.author: Pickthall
50.text: And that My doom is the dolorous doom.

50.author: Qarai
50.text: and that My punishment is a painful punishment.

50.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
50.text: and that My punishment is the painful chastisement.

50.author: Saheeh International
50.text: And that it is My punishment which is the painful punishment.

50.author: Sarwar
50.text: and that My punishment is a painful one.

50.author: Shakir
50.text: And that My punishment-- that is the painful punishment.

50.author: Wahiduddin Khan
50.text: and that My punishment is a painful punishment.

50.author: Yusuf Ali
50.text: And that My Penalty will be indeed the most grievous Penalty.

50.author: Transliteration
50.text: Wa anna AAathabee huwa alAAathabu alaleemu

51.author: Ahmed Ali
51.text: Inform them about the matter of Abraham's guests.

51.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
51.text: And inform them of Ibrahim’s guests.

51.author: Arberry
51.text: And tell them of the guests of Abraham,

51.author: Daryabadi
51.text: And declare thou unto them of Ibrahim's guests.

51.author: Hilali & Khan
51.text: And tell them about the guests (the angels) of Ibrahim (Abraham).

51.author: Itani
51.text: And inform them of the guests of Abraham.

51.author: Maududi
51.text: And tell them about Abraham's guests.

51.author: Mubarakpuri
51.text: And tell them about the guests (i.e., the angels) of Ibrahim.

51.author: Pickthall
51.text: And tell them of Abraham's guests,

51.author: Qarai
51.text: And inform them about the guests of Abraham,

51.author: Saheeh International
51.text: And inform them about the guests of Abraham,

51.author: Sarwar
51.text: Tell them about the guests of Abraham

51.author: Shakir
51.text: And inform them of the guests of Ibrahim:

51.author: Yusuf Ali
51.text: Tell them about the guests of Abraham.

52.author: Ahmed Ali
52.text: When they came to him and said: "Peace," he answered: "Truly we are afraid of you."

52.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
52.text: When the angels came to him and said, “Peace"; he said, “We feel afraid of you.”

52.author: Arberry
52.text: when they entered unto him, saying, 'Peace!' He said, 'Behold, we are afraid of you.'

52.author: Daryabadi
52.text: When they entered unto him, and said; peace! He said: verily we are afraid of you.

52.author: Hilali & Khan
52.text: When they entered unto him, and said: Salaman (peace)! [Ibrahim (Abraham)] said: "Indeed! We are afraid of you."

52.author: Itani
52.text: When they entered upon him, and said, “Peace.” He said, “We are wary of you.”

52.author: Maududi
52.text: When they came to Abraham they said: "Peace be upon you!" He replied: "Indeed we feel afraid of you."

52.author: Mubarakpuri
52.text: When they entered upon him, and said: "Salaman (peace!)." He said: "Indeed we are frightened of you."

52.author: Pickthall
52.text: (How) when they came in unto him, and said: Peace. He said: Lo! we are afraid of you.

52.author: Qarai
52.text: when they entered into his presence and said, ‘Peace!’ He said, ‘We are indeed afraid of you.’

52.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
52.text: They entered to him and said: 'Peace' but he replied: 'We are afraid of you'

52.author: Saheeh International
52.text: When they entered upon him and said, "Peace." [Abraham] said, "Indeed, we are fearful of you."

52.author: Sarwar
52.text: who came to him saying, "Peace be with you." Abraham said, "We are afraid of you".

52.author: Shakir
52.text: When they entered upon him, they said, Peace. He said: Surely we are afraid of you.

52.author: Wahiduddin Khan
52.text: when they came to him they greeted him with: "Peace." He said,"We feel afraid of you."

52.author: Yusuf Ali
52.text: When they entered his presence and said, "Peace!" He said, "We feel afraid of you!"

52.author: Transliteration
52.text: Ith dakhaloo AAalayhi faqaloo salaman qala inna minkum wajiloona

53.author: Ahmed Ali
53.text: "Have no fear," they said. "We bring you news of a son full of wisdom."

53.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
53.text: They said, “Do not fear – we convey to you the glad tidings of a knowledgeable boy.”

53.author: Arberry
53.text: They said, 'Be not afraid; behold, we give thee good tidings of a cunning boy.'

53.author: Daryabadi
53.text: They said: be not afraid; verily we bear thou the glad tidings of a boy knowing.

53.author: Hilali & Khan
53.text: They (the angels) said: "Do not be afraid! We give you glad tidings of a boy (son) possessing much knowledge and wisdom."

53.author: Itani
53.text: They said, “Do not fear; we bring you good news of a boy endowed with knowledge.”

53.author: Maududi
53.text: They said: "Do not feel afraid, for we give you the good news of a wise boy."

53.author: Mubarakpuri
53.text: They said: "Do not be afraid! We bring you the good news of a boy possessing much knowledge and wisdom."

53.author: Pickthall
53.text: They said: Be not afraid! Lo! we bring thee good tidings of a boy possessing wisdom.

53.author: Qarai
53.text: They said, ‘Do not be afraid. Indeed we give you the good news of a wise son.’

53.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
53.text: 'Do not be afraid' they answered. 'We come to give you glad tidings of a knowledgeable child'

53.author: Saheeh International
53.text: [The angels] said, "Fear not. Indeed, we give you good tidings of a learned boy."

53.author: Sarwar
53.text: They replied, "Do not be afraid. We have brought you the glad news of (the birth) of a learned son".

53.author: Shakir
53.text: They said: Be not afraid, surely we give you the good news of a boy, possessing knowledge.

53.author: Wahiduddin Khan
53.text: They said, "Do not be afraid. We come to you with good news. You shall have a son who shall be endowed with great knowledge."

53.author: Yusuf Ali
53.text: They said: "Fear not! We give thee glad tidings of a son endowed with wisdom."

53.author: Transliteration
53.text: Qaloo la tawjal inna nubashshiruka bighulamin AAaleemin

54.author: Ahmed Ali
54.text: "You bring me the good news now," he said, "when old age has come upon me. What good news are you giving me then?"

54.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
54.text: He said, “Do you convey to me the glad tidings upon old age reaching me? So upon what do you convey glad tidings?” (* Prophet Ibrahim said this out of surprise.)

54.author: Arberry
54.text: He said, 'What, do you give me good tidings, though old age has smitten me? Of what do you

54.author: Daryabadi
54.text: He said: bear ye me glad tidings when old age hath touched me? of what then ye bear me glad tidings?

54.author: Hilali & Khan
54.text: [Ibrahim (Abraham)] said: "Do you give me glad tidings (of a son) when old age has overtaken me? Of what then is your news?"

54.author: Itani
54.text: He said, “Do you bring me good news, when old age has overtaken me? What good news do you bring?”

54.author: Maududi
54.text: Abraham said: "What, do you give me this tiding though old age has smitten me? Just consider what tiding do you give me!"

54.author: Mubarakpuri
54.text: He said: "Do you give me this good news while old age has overtaken me Of what then is your news about"

54.author: Pickthall
54.text: He said: Bring ye me good tidings (of a son) when old age hath overtaken me? Of what then can ye bring good tidings?

54.author: Qarai
54.text: He said, ‘Do you give me good news though old age has befallen me? What is the good news that you bring me?’

54.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
54.text: He said: 'What is this, do you bring me glad tidings even though I am old' Of what do you give me glad tidings'

54.author: Saheeh International
54.text: He said, "Have you given me good tidings although old age has come upon me? Then of what [wonder] do you inform?"

54.author: Sarwar
54.text: Abraham asked, "Are you giving me the glad news of a son in my old age? What reason can you give for such glad news?"

54.author: Shakir
54.text: He said: Do you give me good news (of a son) when old age has come upon me?-- Of what then do you give me good news!

54.author: Wahiduddin Khan
54.text: He said, "Do you bring me such news despite my old age? What kind of good news are you bringing me?"

54.author: Yusuf Ali
54.text: He said: "Do ye give me glad tidings that old age has seized me? Of what, then, is your good news?"

54.author: Transliteration
54.text: Qala abashshartumoonee AAala an massaniya alkibaru fabima tubashshirooni

55.author: Ahmed Ali
55.text: "We have given you the happy tidings of a truth," they replied. "So do not be one of those who despair."

55.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
55.text: They said, “We have given you true tidings, therefore do not lose hope.”

55.author: Arberry
55.text: give me good tidings?' They said, 'We give thee good tidings of truth. Be not of those that despair.'

55.author: Daryabadi
55.text: They said: we bear tidings of a truth; be then thou not of the desponding.

55.author: Hilali & Khan
55.text: They (the angels) said: "We give you glad tidings in truth. So be not of the despairing ones."

55.author: Itani
55.text: They said, “We bring you good news in truth, so do not despair.”

55.author: Maududi
55.text: They said: "The good tiding we give you is of truth. Do not, therefore, be of those who despair."

55.author: Mubarakpuri
55.text: They said: "We give you good news in truth. So do not be of those who despair."

55.author: Pickthall
55.text: They said: We bring thee good tidings in truth. So be not thou of the despairing.

55.author: Qarai
55.text: They said, ‘We bring you good news in truth; so do not be despondent.’

55.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
55.text: They replied: 'In truth we have given you glad tidings, do not be one of those who despair'

55.author: Saheeh International
55.text: They said, "We have given you good tidings in truth, so do not be of the despairing."

55.author: Sarwar
55.text: They said, "We have given you the glad news for a true reason so do not despair".

55.author: Shakir
55.text: They said: We give you good news with truth, therefore be not of the despairing.

55.author: Wahiduddin Khan
55.text: They said, "We have, indeed, given you glad tidings in truth; do not therefore despair."

55.author: Yusuf Ali
55.text: They said: "We give thee glad tidings in truth: be not then in despair!"

55.author: Transliteration
55.text: Qaloo bashsharnaka bialhaqqi fala takun mina alqaniteena

56.author: Ahmed Ali
56.text: "Who would despair of the mercy of his Lord," he answered, "but those who go astray."

56.author: Ahmed Raza Khan

70.author: Sarwar
70.text: They replied, "Did we not forbid you to bring anyone to your house?"

70.author: Shakir
70.text: They said: Have we not forbidden you from (other) people?

70.author: Wahiduddin Khan
70.text: They said, "Did we not forbid you to extend hospitality to strangers?"

70.author: Yusuf Ali
70.text: They said: "Did we not forbid thee (to speak) for all and sundry?"

71.author: Ahmed Ali
71.text: "Here are my daughters," said Lot, "if you are so active."

71.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
71.text: He said, “These women of our tribe are my daughters, if you have to.”

71.author: Arberry
71.text: He said, 'These are my daughters, if you would be doing.'

71.author: Daryabadi
71.text: He said: these are my daughters, if act ye must.

71.author: Hilali & Khan
71.text: [Lout (Lot)] said: "These (the girls of the nation) are my daughters (to marry lawfully), if you must act (so)."

71.author: Itani
71.text: He said, “These are my daughters, if you must.”

71.author: Maududi
71.text: Lot exclaimed in exasperation: "If you are bent on doing something, then here are my daughters."

71.author: Mubarakpuri
71.text: [Lut] said: "These are my daughters, if you must act (so)."

71.author: Pickthall
71.text: He said: Here are my daughters, if ye must be doing (so).

71.author: Qarai
71.text: He said, ‘These are my daughters, [marry them] if you should do anything.’

71.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
71.text: He said: 'Here are my daughters; take them (in marriage), if you would be doing'

71.author: Saheeh International
71.text: [Lot] said, "These are my daughters - if you would be doers [of lawful marriage]."

71.author: Sarwar
71.text: Lot said, "These are my daughters if you want them."

71.author: Shakir
71.text: He said: These are my daughters, if you will do (aught).

71.author: Wahiduddin Khan
71.text: He said, "Here are my daughters, if you must act in this way."

71.author: Yusuf Ali
71.text: He said: "There are my daughters (to marry), if ye must act (so)."

71.author: Transliteration
71.text: Qala haolai banatee in kuntum faAAileena

72.author: Ahmed Ali
72.text: Verily by your life they were utterly confused in their (lustful) drunkenness.

72.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
72.text: By your life O dear Prophet (Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) – they are indeed straying in their intoxication.

72.author: Arberry
72.text: By thy life, they wandered blindly in their dazzlement,

72.author: Daryabadi
72.text: By thy life, in their intoxication they were wandering bewildered.

72.author: Hilali & Khan
72.text: Verily, by your life (O Muhammad SAW), in their wild intoxication, they were wandering blindly.

72.author: Itani
72.text: By your life, they were blundering in their drunkenness.

72.author: Maududi
72.text: By your life, (O Prophet), they went about blindly stumbling in their intoxication.

72.author: Mubarakpuri
72.text: Verily, by your life, in their wild intoxication, they were wandering blindly.

72.author: Pickthall
72.text: By thy life (O Muhammad) they moved blindly in the frenzy of approaching death.

72.author: Qarai
72.text: By your life, they were bewildered in their drunkenness.

72.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
72.text: By your life, they wandered blindly in their dazzlement.

72.author: Saheeh International
72.text: By your life, [O Muhammad], indeed they were, in their intoxication, wandering blindly.

72.author: Sarwar
72.text: By your life! In their drunkenness they were truly blind.

72.author: Shakir
72.text: By your life! they were blindly wandering on in their intoxication.

72.author: Wahiduddin Khan
72.text: By your life, they wandered on in their wild intoxication

72.author: Yusuf Ali
72.text: Verily, by thy life (O Prophet), in their wild intoxication, they wander in distraction, to and fro.

73.author: Ahmed Ali
73.text: So they were seized by the mighty blast at break of day;

73.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
73.text: Therefore the scream overcame them at sunrise.

73.author: Arberry
73.text: and the Cry seized them at the sunrise,

73.author: Daryabadi
73.text: Then the shout took hold of them at the sunrise.

73.author: Hilali & Khan
73.text: So As-Saihah (torment - awful cry, etc.) overtook them at the time of sunrise;

73.author: Itani
73.text: So the Blast struck them at sunrise.

73.author: Maududi
73.text: Then the mighty Blast caught them at sunrise,

73.author: Mubarakpuri
73.text: So the Sayhah overtook them at the time of sunrise.

73.author: Pickthall
73.text: Then the (Awful) Cry overtook them at the sunrise.

73.author: Qarai
73.text: So the Cry seized them at sunrise,

73.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
73.text: At sunrise a dreadful Shout seized them.

73.author: Saheeh International
73.text: So the shriek seized them at sunrise.

73.author: Sarwar
73.text: An explosion struck them at sunrise.

73.author: Shakir
73.text: So the rumbling overtook them (while) entering upon the time of sunrise;

73.author: Wahiduddin Khan
73.text: and thereupon the blast [of Our punishment] overtook them at sunrise.

73.author: Yusuf Ali
73.text: But the (mighty) Blast overtook them before morning,

74.author: Ahmed Ali
74.text: And We turned the city upside down, and rained on them stones of hardened lava.

74.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
74.text: And We turned the township upside down and rained upon them stones of heated clay.

74.author: Arberry
74.text: and We turned it uppermost nethermost and rained on it stones of baked clay.

74.author: Daryabadi
74.text: And We made the upside there of downwards, and We rained on them stones of baked clay.

74.author: Hilali & Khan
74.text: And We turned (the towns of Sodom in Palestine) upside down and rained down on them stones of baked clay.

74.author: Itani
74.text: And We turned it upside down, and rained down upon them stones of baked clay.

74.author: Maududi
74.text: and turned the land upside down, and rained down stones of baked clay.

74.author: Mubarakpuri
74.text: And We turned them upside down and rained stones of baked clay upon them.

74.author: Pickthall
74.text: And We utterly confounded them, and We rained upon them stones of heated clay.

74.author: Qarai
74.text: and We made its topmost part its nethermost, and rained on them stones of shale.

74.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
74.text: We laid it (the city) upside down and rained stones of baked clay upon them.

74.author: Saheeh International
74.text: And We made the highest part [of the city] its lowest and rained upon them stones of hard clay.

74.author: Sarwar
74.text: We turned the town upside-down and showered on them lumps of baked clay.

74.author: Shakir
74.text: Thus did We turn it upside down, and rained down upon them stones of what had been decreed.

74.author: Wahiduddin Khan
74.text: We turned the town upside down and rained upon them stones of clay.

74.author: Yusuf Ali
74.text: And We turned (the cities) upside down, and rained down on them brimstones hard as baked clay.

74.author: Transliteration
74.text: FajaAAalna AAaliyaha safilaha waamtarna AAalayhim hijaratan min sijjeelin

75.author: Ahmed Ali
75.text: Herein are really signs for those who discern.

75.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
75.text: Indeed in this are signs for people who perceive.

75.author: Arberry
75.text: Surely in that are signs for such as mark;

75.author: Daryabadi
75.text: Verily therein are signs for men of sagacity.

75.author: Hilali & Khan
75.text: Surely! In this are signs, for those who see (or understand or learn the lessons from the Signs of Allah).

75.author: Itani
75.text: Surely in that are lessons for those who read signs.

75.author: Maududi
75.text: There are great Signs in this for those endowed with intelligence.

75.author: Mubarakpuri
75.text: Surely, in this are signs for those who see.

75.author: Pickthall
75.text: Lo! therein verily are portents for those who read the signs.

75.author: Qarai
75.text: There are indeed signs in that for the percipient.

75.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
75.text: Surely, in that there are signs for those who contemplate.

75.author: Saheeh International
75.text: Indeed in that are signs for those who discern.

75.author: Sarwar
75.text: In this there is evidence (of the Truth) for the prudent ones.

75.author: Shakir
75.text: Surely in this are signs for those who examine.

75.author: Wahiduddin Khan
75.text: There are certainly signs in that for those who can learn a lesson --

75.author: Yusuf Ali
75.text: Behold! in this are Signs for those who by tokens do understand.

76.author: Ahmed Ali
76.text: This (city) lies on a road that still survives.

76.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
76.text: And indeed that township is upon a road still in use.

76.author: Arberry
76.text: surely it is on a way yet remaining;

76.author: Daryabadi
76.text: And verily they are on a pathway lasting.

76.author: Hilali & Khan
76.text: And verily! They (the cities) were right on the highroad (from Makkah to Syria i.e. the place where the Dead Sea is now).

76.author: Itani
76.text: And it is on an existing road.

76.author: Maududi
76.text: The place (where this occurred) lies along a known route.

76.author: Mubarakpuri
76.text: And verily, they were right on the highroad.

76.author: Pickthall
76.text: And lo! it is upon a road still uneffaced.

76.author: Qarai
76.text: Indeed it is on a standing road,

76.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
76.text: Indeed, it is on a way which still exists.

76.author: Saheeh International
76.text: And indeed, those cities are [situated] on an established road.

76.author: Sarwar
76.text: That town lies on a road which still exists.

76.author: Shakir
76.text: And surely it is on a road that still abides.

76.author: Wahiduddin Khan
76.text: it is still there on the highway --

76.author: Yusuf Ali
76.text: And the (cities were) right on the high-road.

77.author: Ahmed Ali
77.text: Indeed there is a portent in this for those who believe.

77.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
77.text: Indeed in this are signs for the believers.

77.author: Arberry
77.text: surely in that is a sign for believers.

77.author: Daryabadi
77.text: Verily in that is a sign for the believers.

77.author: Hilali & Khan
77.text: Surely! Therein is indeed a sign for the believers.

77.author: Itani
77.text: Surely in that is a sign for the believers.

77.author: Maududi
77.text: Verily there is a Sign in this for the believers.

77.author: Mubarakpuri
77.text: Surely, there is indeed a sign in that for the believers.

77.author: Pickthall
77.text: Lo! therein is indeed a portent for believers.

77.author: Qarai
77.text: and there is indeed a sign in that for the faithful.

77.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
77.text: Surely, in that there is a sign for those who believe.

77.author: Saheeh International
77.text: Indeed in that is a sign for the believers.

77.author: Sarwar
77.text: In this there is evidence (of the Truth) for the believers.

77.author: Shakir
77.text: Most surely there is a sign in this for the believers.

77.author: Wahiduddin Khan
77.text: surely in this there is a sign for those who believe.

77.author: Yusuf Ali
77.text: Behold! in this is a sign for those who believed.

78.author: Ahmed Ali
78.text: The dwellers of the Wood (near Midian) were also wicked.

78.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
78.text: And indeed the Dwellers of the Woods were unjust.

78.author: Arberry
78.text: Certainly the dwellers in the Thicket were evildoers,

78.author: Daryabadi
78.text: And the dwellers of the wood surely were wrong-doers.

78.author: Hilali & Khan
78.text: And the dwellers in the wood [i.e. the people of Madyan (Midian) to whom Prophet Shu'aib was sent by Allah), were also Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers, etc.).

78.author: Itani
78.text: The people of the Woods were also wrongdoers.

78.author: Maududi
78.text: And the people of Aykah were also wrong-doers.

78.author: Mubarakpuri
78.text: And the Dwellers of Al-Aykah, were also wrongdoers.

78.author: Pickthall
78.text: And the dwellers in the wood indeed were evil-doers.

78.author: Qarai
78.text: Indeed the inhabitants of Aykah were [also] wrongdoers.

78.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
78.text: The dwellers of the Thicket were harmdoers.

78.author: Saheeh International
78.text: And the companions of the thicket were [also] wrongdoers.

78.author: Sarwar
78.text: Since the People of the Forest were unjust,

78.author: Shakir
78.text: And the dwellers of the thicket also were most surely unjust.

78.author: Wahiduddin Khan
78.text: The people of the Wood were also surely wrongdoers.

78.author: Yusuf Ali
78.text: And the Companions of the Wood were also wrong-doers;

79.author: Ahmed Ali
79.text: So We punished them too. They are both situated by the highway, clearly visible.

79.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
79.text: We therefore took revenge from them; and indeed both these townships are situated on an open road.

79.author: Arberry
79.text: and We took vengeance on them. The two of them were upon a roadway manifest.

79.author: Daryabadi
79.text: So We took vengeance on them. And verily both are on a high-road open.

79.author: Hilali & Khan
79.text: So, We took vengeance on them. They are both on an open highway, plain to see.

79.author: Itani
79.text: So We took revenge upon them. Both are clearly documented.

79.author: Maududi
79.text: So We chastised them. The desolate locations of both communities lie on a well-known highway.

79.author: Mubarakpuri
79.text: So, We took vengeance on them. They are both on an open route, plain to see.

79.author: Pickthall
79.text: So we took vengeance on them; and lo! they both are on a high-road plain to see.

79.author: Qarai
79.text: So We took vengeance on them, and indeed the two of them are on an open highway.

79.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
79.text: On them, too, We took vengeance, and they are both on a clear roadway.

79.author: Saheeh International
79.text: So We took retribution from them, and indeed, both [cities] are on a clear highway.

79.author: Sarwar
79.text: We afflicted them with punishment. Both people had clear (divine) authority among them.

79.author: Shakir
79.text: So We inflicted retribution on them, and they are both, indeed, on an open road (still) pursued.

79.author: Wahiduddin Khan
79.text: So We took vengeance on them. Both are still there on the highway, plain for all to see.

79.author: Yusuf Ali
79.text: So We exacted retribution from them. They were both on an open highway, plain to see.

80.author: Ahmed Ali
80.text: The people of Al-Hijr denied Our apostles;

80.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
80.text: And the people of the Hijr (rocks) denied the Noble Messengers.

80.author: Arberry
80.text: The dwellers in El-Hijr cried lies to the Envoys.

80.author: Daryabadi
80.text: And assuredly the dwellers of Hijr belied the sent ones.

80.author: Hilali & Khan
80.text: And verily, the dwellers of Al-Hijr (the rocky tract) denied the Messengers.

80.author: Itani
80.text: The people of the Rock also rejected the messengers.

80.author: Maududi
80.text: Surely the people of al-Hijr also rejected the Messengers, calling them liars.

80.author: Mubarakpuri
80.text: And verily, the Dwellers of Al-Hijr denied the Messengers.

80.author: Pickthall
80.text: And the dwellers in Al-Hijr denied (Our) messengers.

80.author: Qarai
80.text: Certainly the inhabitants of Hijr denied the apostles.

80.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
80.text: And the dwellers of Al Hijr also belied the Messengers.

80.author: Saheeh International
80.text: And certainly did the companions of Thamud deny the messengers.

80.author: Sarwar
80.text: People of Hijr rejected the Messengers.

80.author: Shakir
80.text: And the dwellers of the Rock certainly rejected the apostles;

80.author: Wahiduddin Khan
80.text: The people of al-Hijr also rejected Our messengers:

80.author: Yusuf Ali
80.text: The Companions of the Rocky Tract also rejected the messengers:

81.author: Ahmed Ali
81.text: And though We had given them Our signs they turned away from them.

81.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
81.text: And We gave them Our signs, and they remained averse to them.

81.author: Arberry
81.text: We brought them. Our signs, and they turned away from them.

81.author: Daryabadi
81.text: And We brought Our signs unto them, yet they were averting themselves therefrom.

81.author: Hilali & Khan
81.text: And We gave them Our Signs, but they were averse to them.

81.author: Itani
81.text: We gave them Our revelations, but they turned away from them.

81.author: Maududi
81.text: We also gave them Our Signs, yet they turned away from them.

81.author: Mubarakpuri
81.text: And We gave them Our signs, but they were averse to them.

81.author: Pickthall
81.text: And we gave them Our revelations, but they were averse to them.

81.author: Qarai
81.text: We had given them Our signs but they disregarded them.

81.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
81.text: We brought them signs, but they turned away.

81.author: Saheeh International
81.text: And We gave them Our signs, but from them they were turning away.

81.author: Sarwar
81.text: We showed them miracles but they ignored them.

81.author: Shakir
81.text: And We gave them Our communications, but they turned aside from them;

81.author: Wahiduddin Khan
81.text: We gave them Our signs, but they turned away from them.

81.author: Yusuf Ali
81.text: We sent them Our Signs, but they persisted in turning away from them.

82.author: Ahmed Ali
82.text: They used to hew dwellings in the mountains to live in security.

82.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
82.text: And they used to carve dwellings in the hills, without fear.

82.author: Arberry
82.text: They were hewing the mountains into houses, therein dwelling securely;

82.author: Daryabadi
82.text: And they were hewing out houses from mountains feeling secure.

82.author: Hilali & Khan
82.text: And they used to hew out dwellings from the mountains (feeling themselves) secure.

82.author: Itani
82.text: They used to carve homes in the mountains, feeling secure.

82.author: Maududi
82.text: They used to hew out houses from the mountains and lived in security.

82.author: Mubarakpuri
82.text: And they used to hew out dwellings from the mountains, (feeling) secure.

82.author: Pickthall
82.text: And they used to hew out dwellings from the hills, (wherein they dwelt) secure.

82.author: Qarai
82.text: They used to hew out dwellings from mountains feeling secure.

82.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
82.text: They hewed their dwellings out of the mountains in safety.

82.author: Saheeh International
82.text: And they used to carve from the mountains, houses, feeling secure.

82.author: Sarwar
82.text: They would carve secure houses out of the mountains.

82.author: Shakir
82.text: And they hewed houses in the mountains in security.

82.author: Wahiduddin Khan
82.text: They carved out dwellings in the mountains, and lived in security --

82.author: Yusuf Ali
82.text: Out of the mountains did they hew (their) edifices, (feeling themselves) secure.

82.author: Transliteration
82.text: Wakanoo yanhitoona mina aljibali buyootan amineena

83.author: Ahmed Ali
83.text: But they were seized by the mighty blast towards the morning;

83.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
83.text: So the scream overcame them at morning.

83.author: Arberry
83.text: and the Cry seized them in the morning;

83.author: Daryabadi
83.text: Then the shout took hold of them in the early morn.

83.author: Hilali & Khan
83.text: But As-Saihah (torment - awful cry etc.) overtook them in the early morning (of the fourth day of their promised punishment days).

83.author: Itani
83.text: But the Blast struck them in the morning.

83.author: Maududi
83.text: Then the Blast caught them in the morning

83.author: Mubarakpuri
83.text: But the Sayhah (torment - awful cry) overtook them in the early morning.

83.author: Pickthall
83.text: But the (Awful) Cry overtook them at the morning hour,

83.author: Qarai
83.text: So the Cry seized them at dawn,

83.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
83.text: But the Shout seized them in the morning,

83.author: Saheeh International
83.text: But the shriek seized them at early morning.

83.author: Sarwar
83.text: An explosion struck them in the morning.

83.author: Shakir
83.text: So the rumbling overtook them in the morning;

83.author: Wahiduddin Khan
83.text: the blast overtook them early one morning.

83.author: Yusuf Ali
83.text: But the (mighty) Blast seized them of a morning,

84.author: Ahmed Ali
84.text: And all that they had done (for security) availed them nothing.

84.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
84.text: Therefore their earnings did not in the least benefit them.

84.author: Arberry
84.text: that they earned did not avail them.

84.author: Daryabadi
84.text: Then availed them not that which they had been earning.

84.author: Hilali & Khan
84.text: And all that which they used to earn availed them not.

84.author: Itani
84.text: All they had acquired was of no avail to them.

84.author: Maududi
84.text: and whatever they had been earning proved of no avail.

84.author: Mubarakpuri
84.text: And all that they used to earn availed them not.

84.author: Pickthall
84.text: And that which they were wont to count as gain availed them not.

84.author: Qarai
84.text: and what they used to earn did not avail them.

84.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
84.text: and that which they had earned did not help them.

84.author: Saheeh International
84.text: So nothing availed them [from] what they used to earn.

84.author: Sarwar
84.text: Out of what they had gained, nothing proved to be of any benefit to them.

84.author: Shakir
84.text: And what they earned did not avail them.

84.author: Wahiduddin Khan
84.text: All that they had acquired was of no avail to them.

84.author: Yusuf Ali
84.text: And of no avail to them was all that they did (with such art and care)!

84.author: Transliteration
84.text: Fama aghna AAanhum ma kanoo yaksiboona

85.author: Ahmed Ali
85.text: We have not created but with reason the heavens and the earth and all that lies within them. The Hour (of the great change) is certain to come. So turn away (from them) with a grace.

85.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
85.text: And We have not created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, except with the Truth; and indeed the Last Day will come, therefore (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), forbear graciously.

85.author: Arberry
85.text: We created not the heavens and the earth, and all that is between them, save in truth. Surely the Hour is coming; so pardon thou, with a gracious pardoning.

85.author: Daryabadi
85.text: And We have not created the heaven and the earth and that which is in between them save with a purpose. And verily the Hour is surely coming; so overlook thou with a seemly overlooking,

85.author: Hilali & Khan
85.text: And We created not the heavens and the earth and all that is between them except with truth, and the Hour is surely coming, so overlook (O Muhammad SAW), their faults with gracious forgiveness. [This was before the ordainment of Jihad holy fighting in Allah's Cause].

85.author: Itani
85.text: We did not create the heavens and the earth, and what lies between them, except with truth. The Hour is coming, so forgive with gracious forgiveness.

85.author: Maududi
85.text: We have not created the heavens and the earth and all that is in between them except with Truth. Surely the Hour will come. So, (O Muhammad), do graciously overlook them (despite their misdeeds).

85.author: Mubarakpuri
85.text: And We did not create the heavens and the earth and all that is between them except with the truth, and the Hour is surely coming, so overlook their faults with gracious forgiveness.

85.author: Pickthall
85.text: We created not the heavens and the earth and all that is between them save with truth, and lo! the Hour is surely coming. So forgive, (O Muhammad), with a gracious forgiveness.

85.author: Qarai
85.text: We did not create the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them except with consummate wisdom, and indeed the Hour is bound to come. So forbear with a graceful forbearance.

85.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
85.text: We did not create the heavens and the earth, and what is between them except in truth. The Hour is sure to come, therefore, forgive them with a graciouspardon.

85.author: Saheeh International
85.text: And We have not created the heavens and earth and that between them except in truth. And indeed, the Hour is coming; so forgive with gracious forgiveness.

85.author: Sarwar
85.text: We have created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, for a genuine purpose. The Day of Judgment will certainly approach, so (Muhammad) forgive them graciously.

85.author: Shakir
85.text: And We did not create the heavens and the earth and what is between them two but in truth; and the hour is most surely coming, so turn away with kindly forgiveness.

85.author: Wahiduddin Khan
85.text: We have created the heavens and the earth and all that is between the two in accordance with the requirements of truth and wisdom. The Hour is surely coming. So overlook [their faults] with gracious forgiveness.

85.author: Yusuf Ali
85.text: We created not the heavens, the earth, and all between them, but for just ends. And the Hour is surely coming (when this will be manifest). So overlook (any human faults) with gracious forgiveness.

85.author: Transliteration
85.text: Wama khalaqna alssamawati waalarda wama baynahuma illa bialhaqqi wainna alssaAAata laatiyatun faisfahi alssafha aljameela

86.author: Ahmed Ali
86.text: Surely your Lord is the Creator and knows (everything).

86.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
86.text: Indeed your Lord only is the Great Creator, the All Knowing.

86.author: Arberry
86.text: Surely thy Lord, He is the All-creator, the All-knowing.

86.author: Daryabadi
86.text: Verify thy Lord! He is the Great Creator, the Knower.

86.author: Hilali & Khan
86.text: Verily, your Lord is the All-Knowing Creator.

86.author: Itani
86.text: Your Lord is the All-Knowing Creator.

86.author: Maududi
86.text: Your Lord is indeed the Creator of all, the All-Knowing.

86.author: Mubarakpuri
86.text: Verily, your Lord is the Knowing Creator.

86.author: Pickthall
86.text: Lo! Thy Lord! He is the All-Wise Creator.

86.author: Qarai
86.text: Indeed your Lord is the All-creator, the All-knowing.

86.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
86.text: Your Lord is the Creator, the Knower.

86.author: Saheeh International
86.text: Indeed, your Lord - He is the Knowing Creator.

86.author: Sarwar
86.text: Your Lord is certainly the All-knowing Creator.

86.author: Shakir
86.text: Surely your Lord is the Creator of all things, the Knowing.

86.author: Wahiduddin Khan
86.text: Surely your Lord is the All Knowing Creator!

86.author: Yusuf Ali
86.text: For verily it is thy Lord who is the Master-Creator, knowing all things.

87.author: Ahmed Ali
87.text: We have indeed given you the seven oft-repeated (examples), and the majestic Qur'an.

87.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
87.text: And indeed We have given you seven verses that are repeated, and the great Qur’an.

87.author: Arberry
87.text: We have given thee seven of the oft-repeated, and the mighty Koran.

87.author: Daryabadi
87.text: And assuredly We have vouchsafed unto thee seven of the repetitions and the mighty Qur'an.

87.author: Hilali & Khan
87.text: And indeed, We have bestowed upon you seven of Al-Mathani (the seven repeatedly recited Verses), (i.e. Surat Al-Fatiha) and the Grand Quran.

87.author: Itani
87.text: We have given you seven of the pairs, and the Grand Quran.

87.author: Maududi
87.text: We have indeed bestowed on you the seven oft-repeated verses and the Great Qur'an.

87.author: Mubarakpuri
87.text: And indeed, We have bestowed upon you seven of the Mathani, and the Grand Qur'an.

87.author: Pickthall
87.text: We have given thee seven of the oft-repeated (verses) and the great Qur'an.

87.author: Qarai
87.text: Certainly We have given you [the surah of] the seven oft-repeated verses and the great Quran.

87.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
87.text: We have given you the seven dual (verses, Al Fatihah) and the Mighty Holy Reading (Koran).

87.author: Saheeh International
87.text: And We have certainly given you, [O Muhammad], seven of the often repeated [verses] and the great Qur'an.

87.author: Sarwar
87.text: (Muhammad), We have given you the seven most repeated (verses) and the great Quran.

87.author: Shakir
87.text: And certainly We have given you seven of the oft-repeated (verses) and the grand Quran.

87.author: Wahiduddin Khan
87.text: We have given you the seven oft-recited verses and the great Quran.

87.author: Yusuf Ali
87.text: And We have bestowed upon thee the Seven Oft-repeated (verses) and the Grand Qur'an.

87.author: Transliteration
87.text: Walaqad ataynaka sabAAan mina almathanee waalqurana alAAatheema

88.author: Ahmed Ali
88.text: So covet not things We have bestowed on a portion of them to enjoy, and do not grieve for them, and protect those who believe;

88.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
88.text: Do not direct your eyes towards the things We have given to some of their couples to enjoy, and do not at all grieve because of them, and take the Muslims within the folds of your mercy.

88.author: Arberry
88.text: Stretch not thine eyes to that We have given pairs of them to enjoy; and do not sorrow for them, and lower thy wing unto the believers,

88.author: Daryabadi
88.text: Cast not thine eyes toward that which We have let classes of them to enjoy; and grieve not over them, and lower thy wing unto the believers.

88.author: Hilali & Khan
88.text: Look not with your eyes ambitiously at what We have bestowed on certain classes of them (the disbelievers), nor grieve over them. And lower your wings for the believers (be courteous to the fellow-believers).

88.author: Itani
88.text: Do not extend your eyes towards what We have bestowed on some couples of them to enjoy, and do not grieve over them, and lower your wing to the believers.

88.author: Maududi
88.text: Do not even cast your eyes towards the worldly goods We have granted to different kinds of people, nor grieve over the state they are in, but turn your loving attention to the believers instead,

88.author: Mubarakpuri
88.text: Look not with your eyes ambitiously at what We have given to certain classes of them, nor grieve over them. And lower your wings to the believers.

88.author: Pickthall
88.text: Strain not thine eyes toward that which We cause some wedded pairs among them to enjoin, and be not grieved on their account, and lower thy wing (in tenderness) for the believers.

88.author: Qarai
88.text: Do not extend your glance toward what We have provided to certain groups of them, and do not grieve for them, and lower your wing to the faithful,

88.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
88.text: Do not stretch your eyes to that We have given pairs of them to enjoy, nor sorrow for them, and lower your wing to the believers.

88.author: Saheeh International
88.text: Do not extend your eyes toward that by which We have given enjoyment to [certain] categories of the disbelievers, and do not grieve over them. And lower your wing to the believers

88.author: Sarwar
88.text: Do not yearn for other people's property and wives and do not grieve (that they do not believe). Be kind to the believers.

88.author: Shakir
88.text: Do not strain your eyes after what We have given certain classes of them to enjoy, and do not grieve for them, and make yourself gentle to the believers.

88.author: Wahiduddin Khan
88.text: Do not strain your eyes towards the worldly benefits We have bestowed on some of them, nor grieve on their account. Lower your wing of mercy for the believers

88.author: Yusuf Ali
88.text: Strain not thine eyes. (Wistfully) at what We have bestowed on certain classes of them, nor grieve over them: but lower thy wing (in gentleness) to the believers.

88.author: Transliteration
88.text: La tamuddanna AAaynayka ila ma mattaAAna bihi azwajan minhum wala tahzan AAalayhim waikhfid janahaka lilmumineena

89.author: Ahmed Ali
89.text: And say: "I am a distinct warner,"

89.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
89.text: And proclaim, “Indeed I, yes I, am the clear Herald of Warning.”

89.author: Arberry
89.text: and say, 'Surely, I am the manifest warner.'

89.author: Daryabadi
89.text: And say thou: verily I! I am a plain warner.

89.author: Hilali & Khan
89.text: And say: "I am indeed a plain warner."

89.author: Itani
89.text: And say, “I am the clear warner.”

89.author: Maududi
89.text: and clearly tell the unbelievers: "I am most certainly a plain warner,"

89.author: Mubarakpuri
89.text: And say (O Muhammad): "I am indeed a plain warner."

89.author: Pickthall
89.text: And say: Lo! I, even I, am a plain warner,

89.author: Qarai
89.text: and say, ‘I am indeed a manifest warner [of punishment from God],’

89.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
89.text: And say: 'I am the plain warner'

89.author: Saheeh International
89.text: And say, "Indeed, I am the clear warner" -

89.author: Sarwar
89.text: Say, "Indeed, I am simply one who warns."

89.author: Shakir
89.text: And say: Surely I am the plain warner.

89.author: Wahiduddin Khan
89.text: and say, "I am, indeed, a plain warner,"

89.author: Yusuf Ali
89.text: And say: "I am indeed he that warneth openly and without ambiguity,"-

90.author: Ahmed Ali
90.text: Like (those) We had sent to those schismatics who slandered their Books

90.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
90.text: (Of a punishment) Like the one We sent down upon the dividers.

90.author: Arberry
90.text: So We sent it down to the partitioners,

90.author: Daryabadi
90.text: Even as We sent down on the dividers -

90.author: Hilali & Khan
90.text: As We have sent down on the dividers, (Quraish pagans or Jews and Christians).

90.author: Itani
90.text: Just as We sent down to the separatists.

90.author: Maududi
90.text: even as We had sent warning to those who had divided their religion into fragments;

90.author: Mubarakpuri
90.text: As We have sent down on the Muqtasimin (conspiring confederates),

90.author: Pickthall
90.text: Such as We send down for those who make division,

90.author: Qarai
90.text: like that We sent down upon those who split into bands,

90.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
90.text: So We sent it down to the partitioners,

90.author: Saheeh International
90.text: Just as We had revealed [scriptures] to the separators

90.author: Sarwar
90.text: (We have given you the Quran) as We had given (the Bible) to those who divided themselves into groups

90.author: Shakir
90.text: Like as We sent down on the dividers

90.author: Wahiduddin Khan
90.text: such as We send down for those who are divisive,

90.author: Yusuf Ali
90.text: (Of just such wrath) as We sent down on those who divided (Scripture into arbitrary parts),-

91.author: Ahmed Ali
91.text: And severed their Scripture into fragments.

91.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
91.text: Those who broke the Word of Allah into several parts. (Those who broke the Torah / Bible or changed its text – or those who called the Qur’an a fabrication / poetry.)

91.author: Arberry
91.text: who have broken the Koran into fragments.

91.author: Daryabadi
91.text: Those who have made the scripture bits.

91.author: Hilali & Khan
91.text: Who have made the Quran into parts. (i.e. believed in a part and disbelieved in the other).

91.author: Itani
91.text: Those who made the Quran obsolete.

91.author: Maududi
91.text: those who had split up their Qur'an into pieces.

91.author: Mubarakpuri
91.text: Who have made the Qur'an into parts.

91.author: Pickthall
91.text: Those who break the Qur'an into parts.

91.author: Qarai
91.text: who represented the Quran as magic.

91.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
91.text: who have broken the Koran into parts,

91.author: Saheeh International
91.text: Who have made the Qur'an into portions.

91.author: Sarwar
91.text: and also divided the Quran believing in some parts and rejecting others.

91.author: Shakir
91.text: Those who made the Quran into shreds.

91.author: Wahiduddin Khan
91.text: and who have broken the Scripture into fragments --

91.author: Yusuf Ali
91.text: (So also on such) as have made Qur'an into shreds (as they please).

92.author: Ahmed Ali
92.text: By your Lord We shall question them one and all

92.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
92.text: Therefore, by your Lord, We shall question every one of them.

92.author: Arberry
92.text: Now by thy Lord, We shall surely question them all together

92.author: Daryabadi
92.text: By thy Lord, We will question them all.

92.author: Hilali & Khan
92.text: So, by your Lord (O Muhammad SAW), We shall certainly call all of them to account.

92.author: Itani
92.text: By your Lord, we will question them all.

92.author: Maududi
92.text: By your Lord, We will question them all

92.author: Mubarakpuri
92.text: So, by your Lord, We shall certainly call all of them to account.

92.author: Pickthall
92.text: Them, by thy Lord, We shall question, every one,

92.author: Qarai
92.text: By your Lord, We will question them all

92.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
92.text: so by your Lord, We will question them all

92.author: Saheeh International
92.text: So by your Lord, We will surely question them all

92.author: Sarwar
92.text: By the Lord, We will hold them all responsible

92.author: Shakir
92.text: So, by your Lord, We would most certainly question them all,

92.author: Wahiduddin Khan
92.text: by your Lord, We shall question them all

92.author: Yusuf Ali
92.text: Therefore, by the Lord, We will, of a surety, call them to account,

93.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
93.text: About all what they used to do.

93.author: Daryabadi
93.text: For that which they have been working.

93.author: Hilali & Khan
93.text: For all that they used to do.

93.author: Itani
93.text: About what they used to do.

93.author: Maududi
93.text: concerning what they have been doing.

93.author: Mubarakpuri
93.text: For all that they used to do.

93.author: Pickthall
93.text: Of what they used to do.

93.author: Qarai
93.text: concerning what they used to do.

93.author: Saheeh International
93.text: About what they used to do.

93.author: Wahiduddin Khan
93.text: about whatever they had been doing!

94.author: Ahmed Ali
94.text: So declare to them what We have enjoined, and turn away from idolaters.

94.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
94.text: Therefore publicly announce what you are commanded, and turn away from the polytheists.

94.author: Arberry
94.text: So shout that thou art commanded and turn thou away from the idolaters.

94.author: Daryabadi
94.text: Promulgate thou that which thou art commanded, and turn away from the associaters.

94.author: Hilali & Khan
94.text: Therefore proclaim openly (Allah's Message Islamic Monotheism) that which you are commanded, and turn away from Al-Mushrikun (polytheists, idolaters, and disbelievers, etc. - see V. 2:105).

94.author: Itani
94.text: So proclaim openly what you are commanded, and turn away from the polytheists.

94.author: Maududi
94.text: (O Prophet), proclaim what you are commanded, and pay no heed to those who associate others with Allah in His Divinity.

94.author: Mubarakpuri
94.text: Therefore openly proclaim what you have been commanded, and turn away from the idolators.

94.author: Pickthall
94.text: So proclaim that which thou art commanded, and withdraw from the idolaters.

94.author: Qarai
94.text: So proclaim what you have been commanded, and turn away from the polytheists.

94.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
94.text: Proclaim then, what you are commanded and turn away from the unbelievers.

94.author: Saheeh International
94.text: Then declare what you are commanded and turn away from the polytheists.

94.author: Sarwar
94.text: Preach what you have been commanded to and stay away from the pagans.

94.author: Shakir
94.text: Therefore declare openly what you are bidden and turn aside from the polytheists.

94.author: Wahiduddin Khan
94.text: Proclaim openly what you are commanded, and avoid the polytheists.

94.author: Yusuf Ali
94.text: Therefore expound openly what thou art commanded, and turn away from those who join false gods with Allah.

94.author: Transliteration
94.text: FaisdaAA bima tumaru waaAArid AAani almushrikeena

95.author: Ahmed Ali
95.text: We are surely sufficient to deal with those who scoff at you,

95.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
95.text: Indeed We suffice you against these mockers.

95.author: Arberry
95.text: We suffice thee against the mockers,

95.author: Daryabadi
95.text: Verily We will suffice unto thee against the mockers.

95.author: Hilali & Khan
95.text: Truly! We will suffice you against the scoffers.

95.author: Itani
95.text: We are enough for you against the mockers.

95.author: Maududi
95.text: Surely We suffice to deal with those who scoff at you,

95.author: Mubarakpuri
95.text: Truly, We will suffice you against the mockers,

95.author: Pickthall
95.text: Lo! We defend thee from the scoffers,

95.author: Qarai
95.text: Indeed We will suffice you against the deriders

95.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
95.text: We suffice you against those who mock,

95.author: Saheeh International
95.text: Indeed, We are sufficient for you against the mockers

95.author: Sarwar
95.text: We shall help you against those who mock you

95.author: Shakir
95.text: Surely We will suffice you against the scoffers

95.author: Wahiduddin Khan
95.text: We will, surely, suffice you against those who mock,

95.author: Yusuf Ali
95.text: For sufficient are We unto thee against those who scoff,-

96.author: Ahmed Ali
96.text: Who place other gods besides God. They will come to know soon.

96.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
96.text: Those who ascribe another God along with Allah; so they will soon come to know.

96.author: Arberry
96.text: even against those who set up with God another god. Certainly they will soon know!

96.author: Daryabadi
96.text: Who set up along with Allah anot her god; presently they shall know.

96.author: Hilali & Khan
96.text: Who set up along with Allah another ilah (god), they will come to know.

96.author: Itani
96.text: Those who set up another god with God. They will come to know.

96.author: Maududi
96.text: those who set up another deity alongside Allah. They shall soon come to know.

96.author: Mubarakpuri
96.text: Who make another god along with Allah; but they will come to know.

96.author: Pickthall
96.text: Who set some other god along with Allah. But they will come to know.

96.author: Qarai
96.text: —those who set up another deity besides Allah. Soon they will know!

96.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
96.text: and those who set up other gods with Allah, indeed, they will soon know.

96.author: Saheeh International
96.text: Who make [equal] with Allah another deity. But they are going to know.

96.author: Sarwar
96.text: and believe other things to be equal to God. They will soon know the truth.

96.author: Shakir
96.text: Those who set up another god with Allah; so they shall soon know.

96.author: Wahiduddin Khan
96.text: who set up another god with God, but they shall soon learn.

96.author: Yusuf Ali
96.text: Those who adopt, with Allah, another god: but soon will they come to know.

96.author: Transliteration
96.text: Allatheena yajAAaloona maAAa Allahi ilahan akhara fasawfa yaAAlamoona

97.author: Ahmed Ali
97.text: We are well aware that you are disheartened by what they say.

97.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
97.text: And indeed We know that you are disheartened by their speech.

97.author: Arberry
97.text: We know indeed thy breast is straitened by the things they say.

97.author: Daryabadi
97.text: And assuredly We know that thou straitenest thy breast by that which they say.

97.author: Hilali & Khan
97.text: Indeed, We know that your breast is straitened at what they say.

97.author: Itani
97.text: We are aware that your heart is strained by what they say.

97.author: Maududi
97.text: We certainly know that their statements sorely grieve you.

97.author: Mubarakpuri
97.text: Indeed, We know that your breast becomes tight because of what they say.

97.author: Pickthall
97.text: Well know We that thy bosom is oppressed by what they say,

97.author: Qarai
97.text: Certainly We know that you become upset because of what they say.

97.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
97.text: Indeed, We know your chest is straitened by that they say.

97.author: Saheeh International
97.text: And We already know that your breast is constrained by what they say.

97.author: Sarwar
97.text: We certainly know that you feel sad about what they say against you.

97.author: Shakir
97.text: And surely We know that your breast straitens at what they say;

97.author: Wahiduddin Khan
97.text: We do indeed know how your heart is distressed at what they say.

97.author: Yusuf Ali
97.text: We do indeed know how thy heart is distressed at what they say.

97.author: Transliteration
97.text: Walaqad naAAlamu annaka yadeequ sadruka bima yaqooloona

98.author: Ahmed Ali
98.text: But you should glorify your Lord with praises, and be among those who bow in submission;

98.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
98.text: Therefore proclaim the Purity of your Lord with praise, and be of those who prostrate.

98.author: Arberry
98.text: Proclaim thy Lord's praise, and be of those that bow,

98.author: Daryabadi
98.text: So hallow thou the praise of thy Lord, and be thou of the prostrate.

98.author: Hilali & Khan
98.text: So glorify the praises of your Lord and be of those who prostrate themselves (to Him).

98.author: Itani
98.text: So glorify the praise of your Lord, and be among those who bow down.

98.author: Maududi
98.text: When (you feel so) glorify your Lord with His praise and prostrate yourself before Him,

98.author: Mubarakpuri
98.text: So glorify the praises of your Lord and be of those who prostrate themselves (to Him).

98.author: Pickthall
98.text: But hymn the praise of thy Lord, and be of those who make prostration (unto Him).

98.author: Qarai
98.text: So celebrate the praise of your Lord and be among those who prostrate,

98.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
98.text: Exalt with the praise of your Lord and be one of those who prostrate.

98.author: Saheeh International
98.text: So exalt [Allah] with praise of your Lord and be of those who prostrate [to Him].

98.author: Sarwar
98.text: Glorify and praise your Lord and be with those who prostrate themselves before God.

98.author: Shakir
98.text: Therefore celebrate the praise of your Lord, and be of those who make obeisance.

98.author: Wahiduddin Khan
98.text: But glorify your Lord with His praise, and prostrate yourself:

98.author: Yusuf Ali
98.text: But celebrate the praises of thy Lord, and be of those who prostrate themselves in adoration.

98.author: Transliteration
98.text: Fasabbih bihamdi rabbika wakun mina alssajideena

99.author: Ahmed Ali
99.text: And go on worshipping your Lord till the certainty (of death) comes upon you.

99.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
99.text: And keep worshipping your Lord, till death* comes to you. (* The certainty.)

99.author: Arberry
99.text: and serve thy Lord, until the Certain comes to thee.

99.author: Daryabadi
99.text: And worship thy Lord until there cometh unto thee the certainty.

99.author: Hilali & Khan
99.text: And worship your Lord until there comes unto you the certainty (i.e. death).

99.author: Itani
99.text: And worship your Lord in order to attain certainty.

99.author: Maududi
99.text: and worship your Lord until the last moment (of your life) that will most certainly come.

99.author: Mubarakpuri
99.text: And worship your Lord until the certainty (i.e. death) comes to you.

99.author: Pickthall
99.text: And serve thy Lord till the Inevitable cometh unto thee.

99.author: Qarai
99.text: and worship your Lord until certainty comes to you.

99.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
99.text: Worship your Lord till the certainty (death) comes to you.

99.author: Saheeh International
99.text: And worship your Lord until there comes to you the certainty (death).

99.author: Sarwar
99.text: Worship your Lord until you achieve the ultimate certainty.

99.author: Shakir
99.text: And serve your Lord until there comes to you that which is certain.

99.author: Wahiduddin Khan
99.text: and worship your Lord until what is certain [death] comes to you.

99.author: Yusuf Ali
99.text: And serve thy Lord until there come unto thee the Hour that is Certain.
